metadata.validation: SRS-aware with placeholder blocking and deduplication

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User downloads mobile application from Google Play Store
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can successfully download the mobile application through the Google Play Store.
preconditions: Google Play Store is accessible on the device
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store app on the device
Step 2: Search for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 3: Tap on the search result for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 4: Verify that the application details page is displayed with correct app name and icon
Step 5: Tap on 'Install' button
Step 6: Confirm installation by tapping 'Accept & Install' if prompted
Step 7: Verify that the application opens successfully
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is able to download and open the mobile application from Google Play Store without any errors.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User tries to download non-existent app from Google Play Store
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system handles an error when a user tries to download a non-existent application.
preconditions: Google Play Store is accessible on the device
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store app on the device
Step 2: Search for 'NonExistentApp'
Step 3: Verify that no search results are displayed
Step 4: Tap on the back button to return to the home screen
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: System displays an error message indicating that the app was not found.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User downloads mobile application with minimum price filter
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application filters restaurants based on the minimum price correctly.
preconditions: Google Play Store is accessible on the device
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store app on the device
Step 2: Search for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 3: Tap on the search result for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 4: Navigate to the filter section and set minimum price to $50
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants with prices above $50 are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Only restaurants with prices above $50 are displayed in the search results.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User downloads mobile application and verifies app functionality
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the downloaded mobile application functions correctly by checking its basic features.
preconditions: Google Play Store is accessible on the device
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Play Store app on the device
Step 2: Search for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 3: Tap on the search result for 'RestaurantApp'
Step 4: Install and open the application
Step 5: Verify that the home screen displays a list of restaurants
Step 6: Navigate to a restaurant detail page and verify that all necessary information is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application opens successfully and displays a functional list of restaurants.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch to map view for all search options
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can switch from list view to map view for all search options.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen at /home
Step 2: Click 'Search' button with price range 100-500
Step 3: Verify that search results are displayed in list view by default
Step 4: Click 'Switch View' button located below the search bar
Step 5: Verify that search results are now displayed on a map
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are successfully switched to map view and displayed correctly

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to switch to invalid view type
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to switch to an invalid view type results in an error message.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen at /home
Step 2: Click 'Search' button with price range 100-500
Step 3: Verify that search results are displayed in list view by default
Step 4: Click 'Switch View' button located below the search bar and select an invalid option (e.g., 'Table')
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the selected view type is not supported
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed when attempting to switch to an invalid view type

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch to map view with minimum price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can switch from list view to map view when using the minimum price range.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen at /home
Step 2: Click 'Search' button with price range 0-50
Step 3: Verify that search results are displayed in list view by default
Step 4: Click 'Switch View' button located below the search bar
Step 5: Verify that search results are now displayed on a map
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are successfully switched to map view and displayed correctly with minimum price range

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Switch to map view from another feature (e.g., favorites)
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can switch from list view to map view when navigating from the favorites feature.
preconditions: User is logged in and has added restaurants to favorites
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the favorites screen at /favorites
Step 2: Click on a restaurant listed as a favorite
Step 3: Verify that the restaurant details page is displayed
Step 4: Click 'Switch View' button located below the restaurant details
Step 5: Verify that the restaurant details are now displayed on a map
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurant details are successfully switched to map view and displayed correctly from favorites feature

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application from official website
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application can be downloaded successfully from the official website.
preconditions: User has access to a web browser
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the official website at https://example.com/download
Step 2: Click on the 'Download App' button
Step 3: Verify that the download starts automatically
Step 4: Confirm that the downloaded file is a valid mobile application (e.g., .apk for Android, .ipa for iOS)
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Mobile application downloads successfully and is in a valid format

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to download mobile application from unauthorized website
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application cannot be downloaded from an unauthorized website.
preconditions: User has access to a web browser
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to an unauthorized website at https://fakeexample.com/download
Step 2: Click on the 'Download App' button
Step 3: Verify that the download fails with an error message
Step 4: Confirm that no mobile application file is downloaded
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Download attempt from unauthorized website results in error and no file is downloaded

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to download mobile application using maximum allowed data limit
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application can be downloaded within the maximum allowed data limit.
preconditions: User has access to a web browser with sufficient data allowance
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the official website at https://example.com/download
Step 2: Click on the 'Download App' button
Step 3: Monitor network usage during download
Step 4: Verify that the download completes within the maximum allowed data limit
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Mobile application downloads successfully within the maximum allowed data limit

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Verify mobile application download from social media platform
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application can be downloaded through a social media platform.
preconditions: User has access to a web browser and is logged into a social media account
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to a popular social media platform at https://socialmedia.com
Step 2: Search for the mobile application using keywords 'example app'
Step 3: Click on the download link provided by the social media platform
Step 4: Verify that the download starts automatically and completes successfully
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Mobile application downloads successfully through a social media platform

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User selects information link from search results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can successfully select the information link from search results and is redirected to the correct page.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search results are displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in the search field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the first result item contains an 'Information' link
Step 5: Click on the 'Information' link from the first result item
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to a page displaying detailed restaurant information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempt to select information link with invalid search term
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to select the information link with an invalid search term results in an error message.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search results are displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'InvalidTerm' in the search field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that no search results are displayed
Step 5: Attempt to click on an 'Information' link (expected to fail)
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating no results found is displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select information link from the last result item
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can successfully select the information link from the last result item and is redirected to the correct page.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search results are displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in the search field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the last result item contains an 'Information' link
Step 5: Click on the 'Information' link from the last result item
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to a page displaying detailed restaurant information

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Select information link from search results and verify data consistency with database
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that selecting the information link from search results displays consistent data with the restaurant's details stored in the database.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search results are displayed
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Italian' in the search field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the first result item contains an 'Information' link
Step 5: Click on the 'Information' link from the first result item and verify that all displayed details match those in the database
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: All displayed restaurant details match those stored in the database

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a specific restaurant
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on the information link directs to the correct page with all necessary details.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile application
Step 2: Scroll down and find a restaurant named 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 3: Tap on the information link next to 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 4: Verify that the page displays a picture of 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 5: Confirm that the page includes the restaurant name, address, phone number, email address, type of food, average price, and restaurant description
Step 6: Verify that the menu section is present with dish names, descriptions, and prices
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is directed to the correct information page with all necessary details displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Attempting to access information link without being logged in
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application prevents unauthenticated users from accessing restaurant information pages.
preconditions: User is not logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile application
Step 2: Scroll down and find a restaurant named 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 3: Tap on the information link next to 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 4: Verify that the user is redirected to the login page
Step 5: Confirm that the application displays an error message indicating authentication required
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to login page and sees an error message for unauthenticated access

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Accessing information link with the maximum number of restaurants displayed
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on the information link for a restaurant displays all necessary details even when the maximum number of results is reached.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile application
Step 2: Scroll down and find a restaurant named 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 3: Tap on the information link next to 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 4: Verify that the page displays a picture of 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 5: Confirm that the page includes all necessary details such as name, address, phone number, email address, type of food, average price, and restaurant description
Step 6: Verify that the menu section is present with dish names, descriptions, and prices
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is directed to the correct information page with all necessary details displayed, even when maximum number of results is reached

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Interacting with the information link and other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that clicking on the information link for a restaurant does not interfere with other features such as adding to favorites or sharing.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the home screen
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the home screen of the mobile application
Step 2: Scroll down and find a restaurant named 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 3: Tap on the information link next to 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 4: Verify that the page displays a picture of 'The Gourmet Bistro'
Step 5: Confirm that the page includes all necessary details such as name, address, phone number, email address, type of food, average price, and restaurant description
Step 6: Tap on the 'Add to Favorites' button
Step 7: Verify that the restaurant is added to favorites list
Step 8: Navigate back to home screen and tap on the 'Favorites' tab
Step 9: Confirm that 'The Gourmet Bistro' is listed in the favorites section
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is directed to the correct information page with all necessary details displayed, and can interact with other features such as adding to favorites without interference

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with valid inputs
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly displays restaurants within a specified price range.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with the specified price range
Step 5: Verify that restaurants within the price range $50 to $200 are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within the specified price range are correctly displayed and sorted by distance

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid maximum price
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles an invalid maximum price input gracefully.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '-10' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with the specified invalid price range
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message is displayed for invalid maximum price input

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with minimum and maximum prices equal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles a search where the minimum and maximum prices are equal.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '150' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '150' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with the specified equal prices
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants priced at $150 are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Only restaurants priced at the specified price are displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with distance filter
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly displays restaurants within a specified price range and distance.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter minimum price '50' in the 'Min Price' field
Step 3: Enter maximum price '200' in the 'Max Price' field
Step 4: Select distance filter for 'Within 10 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button with the specified price range and distance filter
Step 6: Verify that restaurants within the price range $50 to $200 and within 10 km are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants within the specified price range and distance are correctly displayed

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with valid distance range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly filters restaurants based on a specified distance range.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'New York' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '5 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '10 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants within 5-10 km of New York are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within the specified distance range (5-10 km) of New York

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with invalid distance range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles an invalid distance range input gracefully.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'New York' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '15 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid distance range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for invalid distance range (minimum greater than maximum)

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with minimum and maximum distances equal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly handles a search when the minimum and maximum distances are set to the same value.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'New York' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '5 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants exactly 5 km from New York are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants exactly 5 km from New York

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with integration to user location
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly uses the user's current location for distance calculations in search results.
preconditions: User is logged in and at the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Ensure the app has access to the user's location
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '0 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants within 5 km of the user's current location are displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within 5 km of the user's current location

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with custom distance range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are displayed based on a custom distance range.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'New York City' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '5 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '10 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that results are displayed within the 5-10 km distance range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within a 5-10 km radius of New York City

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid maximum distance
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search fails when an invalid maximum distance is entered.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'San Francisco' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '10 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '-5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for negative maximum distance

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with minimum and maximum distances equal
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are displayed when minimum and maximum distances are set to the same value.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Los Angeles' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '5 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Verify that results are displayed within a 5 km radius of Los Angeles
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within a 5 km radius of Los Angeles

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with integration to restaurant list page
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results correctly link to the restaurant detail pages.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Chicago' in the destination field
Step 3: Set minimum distance to '0 km'
Step 4: Set maximum distance to '5 km'
Step 5: Click 'Search' button
Step 6: Select a restaurant from the search results
Step 7: Verify that the user is redirected to the restaurant detail page at /restaurant/123
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to a valid restaurant detail page after selecting a result

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with valid integers
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly when a user searches for restaurants within a specific price range using valid integer inputs.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with price range 50-200
Step 5: Verify that results are displayed within the specified price range (50 to 200)
Step 6: Confirm that the search results are sorted by price in ascending order
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within the specified price range (50 to 200) and are sorted by price in ascending order

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by invalid price range with non-integer values
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results display an error message when a user searches for restaurants using non-integer values in the price range fields.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': 'abc' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': 'def' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with invalid price range values
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input for price range
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed indicating invalid input for price range

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by minimum and maximum price with the same value
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results display restaurants when a user searches for restaurants within a specific price range where the minimum and maximum prices are the same.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '150' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '150' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with price range 150-150
Step 5: Verify that results are displayed for restaurants priced exactly at 150
Step 6: Confirm that the search results are sorted by price in ascending order
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants priced exactly at 150 and are sorted by price in ascending order

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by price range with integration to user preferences
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the search results are displayed correctly when a user searches for restaurants within a specific price range and their preferred distance is taken into account.
preconditions: User is logged in, on the search page, and has set a preferred distance of 10 km
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the restaurant search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'min_price': '50' in the minimum price field
Step 3: Enter 'max_price': '200' in the maximum price field
Step 4: Click 'Search' button with price range 50-200
Step 5: Verify that results are displayed within the specified price range (50 to 200)
Step 6: Confirm that the search results are sorted by distance first, then by price, displaying maximum 100 restaurants
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants within the specified price range (50 to 200), sorted by distance first, then by price, displaying maximum 100 restaurants

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search with invalid destination
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects an invalid destination input and prompts for a valid one.
preconditions: User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to search page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'InvalidDestination' in destination field
Step 3: Set price range from '$50' to '$100'
Step 4: Click 'Search' button
Step 5: Verify error message 'Invalid destination. Please enter a valid city.' is displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating invalid destination is shown

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with valid input
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly displays restaurants of a specific type when a valid type is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' screen at /home
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' button located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Select 'Italian' from the list of restaurant types displayed in the search modal
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is filtered to show only Italian restaurants
Step 5: Confirm that the number of displayed restaurants is within the expected range (e.g., between 10 and 20)
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays only Italian restaurants in the search results, with a count between 10 and 20.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with invalid input
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles an invalid restaurant type input gracefully.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' screen at /home
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' button located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Enter an invalid restaurant type, such as 'XYZ', in the search input field
Step 4: Verify that no restaurants are displayed in the search results
Step 5: Confirm that a relevant error message is shown to the user (e.g., 'No restaurants found')
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays an error message indicating no restaurants were found for the invalid restaurant type.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with maximum results
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly displays the maximum number of restaurants when a specific type is selected.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' screen at /home
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' button located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Select 'Italian' from the list of restaurant types displayed in the search modal
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is filtered to show only Italian restaurants
Step 5: Confirm that the number of displayed restaurants is exactly 100
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays exactly 100 Italian restaurants in the search results.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with integration to nearby location
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application correctly displays restaurants of a specific type within a user's current location.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application and has granted location permissions
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Home' screen at /home
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' button located at the top right corner of the screen
Step 3: Select 'Italian' from the list of restaurant types displayed in the search modal
Step 4: Verify that the list of restaurants is filtered to show only Italian restaurants within a reasonable distance (e.g., 5 km)
Step 5: Confirm that the number of displayed restaurants is within the expected range (e.g., between 10 and 20)
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays Italian restaurants within a reasonable distance of the user's current location, with a count between 10 and 20.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a specific dish by name
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for a specific dish and see the correct results.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'Pizza Margherita' in the search input field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that the first result contains 'Pizza Margherita'
Step 5: Confirm that the results are sorted by relevance and displayed within 10 seconds
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User sees the correct dish 'Pizza Margherita' as the first result and results are sorted by relevance.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a non-existent dish
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user receives an appropriate error message when searching for a non-existent dish.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'NonExistentDish' in the search input field
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that no results are displayed
Step 5: Confirm that an error message 'No dishes found' is shown
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an error message indicating no results were found.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Search for a dish with exact price range
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can search for dishes within an exact price range and see the correct results.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page at /search
Step 2: Enter 'price_min': 50, 'price_max': 100 in the search input fields
Step 3: Click 'Search' button
Step 4: Verify that all results have prices between 50 and 100
Step 5: Confirm that the results are displayed within 5 seconds

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User attempts to set language to an invalid option
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles an attempt to set an invalid language option gracefully.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Settings' page by tapping on the gear icon at the bottom of the screen
Step 2: Tap on 'Language' option under the 'General' settings section
Step 3: Attempt to select an invalid language option (e.g., 'InvalidLang') from the list of available languages
Step 4: Verify that a warning message is displayed indicating that the selected language is not supported
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: A warning message is displayed indicating that the selected language is not supported

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User sets language to default option
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that the user can set the application language to the default option and that all text is displayed in the default language.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Settings' page by tapping on the gear icon at the bottom of the screen
Step 2: Tap on 'Language' option under the 'General' settings section
Step 3: Select the default language option (e.g., 'System Default') from the list of available languages
Step 4: Verify that all text in the application is now displayed in the system's default language
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: All text within the mobile application is displayed in the system's default language

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User sets language and verifies integration with other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Verify that setting the application language affects other features such as menu items, order confirmation messages, and error messages.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Settings' page by tapping on the gear icon at the bottom of the screen
Step 2: Tap on 'Language' option under the 'General' settings section
Step 3: Select 'French' from the list of available languages
Step 4: Navigate to the 'Menu' and select an item
Step 5: Place an order for the selected menu item
Step 6: Verify that all text in the order confirmation message is displayed in French
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: All text within the mobile application, including order confirmation messages, is displayed in French

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User successfully creates an account with valid data
phase1_test_cases.description: Verifies that a user can create an account using all required fields with valid input.
preconditions: Navigate to the web-portal home page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Register' page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'john_doe' in username field
Step 3: Enter password 'P@ssw0rd123' in password field
Step 4: Enter address '123 Main St, Anytown, USA' in address field
Step 5: Enter email 'john.doe@example.com' in email field
Step 6: Enter phone number '123-456-7890' in phone number field
Step 7: Enter mobile number '987-654-3210' in mobile number field
Step 8: Click 'Create Account' button
Step 9: Verify redirect to dashboard at /dashboard
Step 10: Verify account creation success message displayed
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: User successfully creates an account and is redirected to the dashboard with a confirmation message.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User fails to create an account due to invalid email format
phase1_test_cases.description: Verifies that the system prevents account creation when the email address is in an invalid format.
preconditions: Navigate to the web-portal home page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Register' page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'jane_doe' in username field
Step 3: Enter password 'P@ssw0rd123' in password field
Step 4: Enter address '456 Elm St, Anytown, USA' in address field
Step 5: Enter email 'jane.doeexample.com' in email field (invalid format)
Step 6: Enter phone number '123-456-7890' in phone number field
Step 7: Enter mobile number '987-654-3210' in mobile number field
Step 8: Click 'Create Account' button
Step 9: Verify error message displayed indicating invalid email format
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed indicating invalid email format, preventing account creation.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: User creates an account with the minimum required fields
phase1_test_cases.description: Verifies that a user can create an account using only the mandatory fields.
preconditions: Navigate to the web-portal home page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to 'Register' page at /register
Step 2: Enter username 'min_user' in username field
Step 3: Enter password 'MinPass123' in password field
Step 4: Click 'Create Account' button without entering other fields
Step 5: Verify error message displayed indicating missing required fields
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed indicating missing required fields, preventing account creation.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Manage information - Positive Scenario
phase1_test_cases.description: Test the main success scenario for managing information.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection Flaws in Download URL
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application does not allow SQL injection or other injection attacks when downloading a mobile application.
preconditions: The mobile app is installed and running on a test device
A valid download URL is available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download' section of the mobile app
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL injection payload in the download URL field, e.g., 'https://example.com/app?download=1; DROP TABLE users;
Step 3: Submit the download request
Step 4: Verify that the app does not execute any harmful actions and displays an error message indicating invalid input
Step 5: Confirm that no data is modified or deleted from the database
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should reject the malicious URL and display an error message, without executing any harmful actions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Unsecured Download Links
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application only allows secure (HTTPS) download links and rejects insecure (HTTP) links.
preconditions: The mobile app is installed and running on a test device
A valid HTTPS download URL is available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download' section of the mobile app
Step 2: Enter an insecure HTTP download link in the download URL field, e.g., 'http://example.com/app.apk'
Step 3: Submit the download request
Step 4: Verify that the app rejects the HTTP link and displays an error message indicating that only secure links are allowed
Step 5: Confirm that no data is modified or deleted from the device
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should reject the insecure HTTP link and display an error message, without initiating any download.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Malicious Download Links
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application does not allow users to download malicious applications.
preconditions: The mobile app is installed and running on a test device
A known malicious APK file is available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download' section of the mobile app
Step 2: Download a known malicious APK file from a trusted source
Step 3: Install the downloaded APK on the test device
Step 4: Verify that the app does not execute any harmful actions and displays an error message indicating that the download is not allowed
Step 5: Confirm that no data is modified or deleted from the device
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should reject the malicious APK and display an error message, without initiating any installation.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: iOS vs Android Compatibility for Download Mobile Application

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be successfully downloaded and installed on both iOS and Android devices without any errors.

Step 5: Verify that the app displays accurate location data.
Step 6: On the Android device, open the Google Play Store and search for 'FoodServiceApp'.

Step 9: Open the app and allow location permissions.
Step 10: Verify that the app displays accurate location data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application - Verify successful download via API
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can be successfully downloaded using the provided API endpoint.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/download?app_id=12345
Step 2: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains download URL for the mobile application
Step 5: Verify the download URL is valid and accessible
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 200 status code with a valid download URL in response body

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application - Verify rate limiting for downloads
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API enforces rate limiting on download requests.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/download?app_id=12345 five times in quick succession
Step 2: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP 429 status code returned for the third request
Step 4: Verify response body contains rate limit exceeded message
Step 5: Wait for one minute and resend the GET request
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 429 status code with rate limit exceeded message after five requests within one minute

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application - Verify authentication failure for download request
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the API returns an error when an unauthenticated user attempts to download the mobile application.
preconditions: No authentication token provided
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/download?app_id=12345
Step 2: Do not include any headers
Step 3: Verify HTTP 401 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains authentication required message
Step 5: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> and resend the request
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: HTTP 401 status code with authentication required message when no token is provided, and HTTP 200 status code with valid token

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create a new order and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the creation of a new order, including mandatory fields, data types, and foreign key relationships.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant manager
Restaurant has at least one menu item available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Orders' page
2. Click on the 'Create New Order' button
3. Enter the customer's name: 'John Doe'
4. Select a menu item from the dropdown: 'Spaghetti Carbonara'
5. Set the quantity to 2
6. Click on the 'Save Order' button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The new order is successfully created with customer name 'John Doe', menu item 'Spaghetti Carbonara', and quantity 2. The order appears in the 'Orders' list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Update an existing order and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the update of an existing order, including mandatory fields, data types, and foreign key relationships.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant manager
There is at least one existing order
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Orders' page
2. Locate and click on an existing order with ID: 12345
3. Update the quantity of the menu item to 3
4. Click on the 'Save Changes' button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The existing order with ID 12345 is successfully updated to have a quantity of 3. The updated order details are reflected in the 'Orders' list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Delete an existing order and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the deletion of an existing order, including foreign key relationships.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant manager
There is at least one existing order
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Orders' page
2. Locate and click on an existing order with ID: 67890
3. Click on the 'Delete Order' button
4. Confirm the deletion
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The existing order with ID 67890 is successfully deleted. The order no longer appears in the 'Orders' list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During App Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app can recover from network interruptions during download.
preconditions: Ensure device is connected to a stable internet connection
Start downloading an application
test_steps: Step 1: Start downloading an application on the mobile device
Step 2: Interrupt the network connection (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi or switch to airplane mode)
Step 3: Wait for the download to fail and show a network error message
Step 4: Re-establish the network connection
Step 5: Verify that the app resumes downloading from where it left off without losing progress
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should resume downloading from the point of interruption and not lose any data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Download Completion
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app can recover from crashes after a download completes.
preconditions: Ensure device is connected to a stable internet connection
Start downloading an application
test_steps: Step 1: Start downloading an application on the mobile device
Step 2: Allow the download to complete
Step 3: Simulate an app crash (e.g., force stop the app from the task manager)
Step 4: Reopen the app
Step 5: Verify that the app displays the downloaded application and allows further actions (e.g., open, install)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from a crash and display the downloaded application without any loss of data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data Persistence Across Crashes During Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app persists data across crashes during a download.
preconditions: Ensure device is connected to a stable internet connection
Start downloading an application
test_steps: Step 1: Start downloading an application on the mobile device
Step 2: Simulate an app crash (e.g., force stop the app from the task manager)
Step 3: Reopen the app and resume the download
Step 4: Verify that the app displays the same progress as before the crash
Step 5: Complete the download and verify that all data is intact
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should persist data across crashes during a download and resume from the last known state.

test_data.actual_field1: Data persistence across crashes during download
test_data.expected_limit: Download resumes from last known state without losing data

Step 4: Verify that the app is installed successfully by opening it from the Home screen or App drawer.
Step 5: On a device with a small screen (e.g., smartphone), repeat steps 1-4.
Step 6: Verify that the app is installed successfully and that all UI elements are visible and functional.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be successfully downloaded and installed on devices with different screen sizes and resolutions without any errors or layout issues.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Type Variations (WiFi, 4G, 5G) Compatibility for Downloading Mobile Application
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can be downloaded and installed on devices with different network types.

Step 5: On the same device, switch to 4G network and repeat steps 1-4.
Step 6: Verify that the app is installed successfully by opening it from the Home screen or App drawer.
Step 7: On the same device, switch to 5G network and repeat steps 1-4.
Step 8: Verify that the app is installed successfully by opening it from the Home screen or App drawer.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application should be successfully downloaded and installed on devices with different network types without any errors.

phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the mobile application can be downloaded and installed successfully.

Device is compatible with the app

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create a new order and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that creating a new order results in accurate data entry, correct foreign key relationships, and transaction integrity.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
Restaurant menu is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Order' page
2. Select a meal from the menu (e.g., 'Spaghetti Carbonara')
3. Enter customer details such as name ('John Doe'), phone number ('1234567890'), and address ('123 Main St, Anytown, USA')
4. Click on 'Place Order' button
5. Verify that the order is created with a unique order ID
6. Check that the order status is set to 'Pending'
7. Confirm that the meal name ('Spaghetti Carbonara') and quantity (1) are correctly displayed in the order summary
8. Verify that the customer details (name, phone number, address) are accurately recorded
9. Ensure that the total price is calculated correctly based on the selected meal and any applicable discounts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order is successfully created with correct meal details, customer information, and accurate total price. Foreign key relationships are maintained.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Update order status and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that updating an order's status results in accurate data entry, correct foreign key relationships, and transaction integrity.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
An existing order exists
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Orders' page
2. Locate and select an existing order (e.g., Order ID: 1001)
3. Click on the 'Update Status' button
4. Change the status from 'Pending' to 'Completed'
5. Verify that the order status is updated to 'Completed'
6. Confirm that the completion timestamp is recorded
7. Check that the order details (meal, quantity, customer information) remain unchanged
8. Ensure that the total price and payment status are still accurate
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order status is updated to 'Completed' with a recorded completion timestamp. Foreign key relationships are maintained.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Delete an order and verify data integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that deleting an order results in accurate data entry, correct foreign key relationships, and transaction integrity.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid credentials
An existing order exists
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Orders' page
2. Locate and select an existing order (e.g., Order ID: 1001)
3. Click on the 'Delete Order' button
4. Confirm deletion by clicking 'Yes'
5. Verify that the order is no longer listed in the orders table
6. Check that the order details are removed from the system
7. Ensure that any related foreign key relationships (e.g., customer, meal) are updated or deleted appropriately
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order is successfully deleted and all related data is removed from the system. Foreign key relationships are maintained.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to 'Recent Orders' and verify loading time
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application loads the 'Recent Orders' view within a specified time limit.
preconditions: User is logged in
User has at least 10 recent orders
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard screen
Step 2: Tap on the 'Orders' tab
Step 3: Scroll down and tap on the 'Recent Orders' view option
Step 4: Measure the time taken for the 'Recent Orders' view to fully load
Step 5: Verify that the loading time is less than or equal to 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Recent Orders' view should load within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to 'Top Dishes' and verify the number of displayed dishes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application displays the correct number of top dishes in the 'Top Dishes' view.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant has at least 50 dishes
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard screen
Step 2: Tap on the 'Menu' tab
Step 3: Scroll down and tap on the 'Top Dishes' view option
Step 4: Count the number of dishes displayed in the 'Top Dishes' view
Step 5: Verify that the number of displayed dishes is exactly 10
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Top Dishes' view should display exactly 10 dishes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to 'Customer Reviews' and verify the average rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application calculates and displays the correct average rating for customer reviews.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant has at least 50 customer reviews
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the main dashboard screen
Step 2: Tap on the 'Reviews' tab
Step 3: Scroll down and tap on the 'Customer Reviews' view option
Step 4: Calculate the average rating of all reviews displayed
Step 5: Verify that the calculated average rating matches the displayed average rating
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Customer Reviews' view should display the correct average rating.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that the application does not allow unauthorized access to switch result view
phase2_test_cases.description: This test validates that only authorized users can switch between different result views in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged out of the application
Valid user credentials are available
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application and navigate to the login screen
Step 2: Enter valid user credentials and log in
Step 3: Navigate to a page that displays result views (e.g., order history)
Step 4: Attempt to switch between different result views using the provided UI elements
Step 5: Verify that all result views are accessible without any authentication prompts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All result views should be accessible after logging in with valid credentials, and no authentication prompts should appear.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that the application does not allow unauthorized users to switch result view using session hijacking
phase2_test_cases.description: This test validates that the mobile application is protected against session hijacking attempts when switching between different result views.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
Session ID is captured from a valid user session
test_steps: Step 1: Capture the session ID of a valid user session
Step 2: Launch the mobile application and navigate to the login screen
Step 3: Enter the captured session ID as if it were a username or password
Step 4: Attempt to switch between different result views using the provided UI elements
Step 5: Verify that all result views are accessible without requiring re-authentication
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All result views should be accessible after providing the captured session ID, and no authentication prompts should appear.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that the application does not allow unauthorized users to switch result view using SQL injection
phase2_test_cases.description: This test validates that the mobile application is protected against SQL injection attacks when switching between different result views.
preconditions: User is logged into the application
SQL injection payload is available
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to a page that displays result views (e.g., order history)
Step 2: Enter an SQL injection payload in the search or filter field for result views
Step 3: Attempt to switch between different result views using the provided UI elements
Step 4: Verify that all result views are accessible without any errors or prompts indicating a security breach
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All result views should be accessible after entering the SQL injection payload, and no errors or prompts indicating a security breach should appear.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view from list to grid
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users can switch between a list and grid view of restaurant results without any errors or delays.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant search results are displayed in list view
test_steps: 1. Tap on the 'Switch View' button located at the top right corner of the screen
2. Observe if the restaurant results transition from a list to a grid format
3. Verify that each restaurant entry now displays as a tile with an image, name, and rating
4. Scroll through the grid view and ensure all restaurants are displayed correctly
5. Tap on any restaurant tile to open its details page
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Switch View' button successfully switches the result view from list to grid format without errors, and all restaurants are displayed correctly in the new grid format.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter restaurant results by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can filter restaurant results by cuisine type and that the filtered list updates correctly.

Restaurant search results are displayed

2. Select a cuisine type from the filter options (e.g., Italian, Chinese)
3. Observe if the restaurant results update to only show restaurants of the selected cuisine type
4. Scroll through the filtered list and ensure all restaurants are of the selected cuisine type

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Filters' icon successfully filters the restaurant results by the selected cuisine type, and only restaurants of that cuisine are displayed in the list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurant results by rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users can sort restaurant results by rating and that the sorted list updates correctly.

Restaurant search results are displayed

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to list mode
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API endpoint for switching result views returns a list of results in list mode.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Result data exists
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/results?view=list with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter view=list
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of results
5. Verify each result in the array includes fields 'id', 'name', and 'description'
6. Verify the total number of results matches the expected limit
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of results in list mode with correct fields and within expected limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to grid mode
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API endpoint for switching result views returns a list of results in grid mode.

Result data exists
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/results?view=grid with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter view=grid
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of results
5. Verify each result in the array includes fields 'id', 'name', and 'description'
6. Verify the total number of results matches the expected limit
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of results in grid mode with correct fields and within expected limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Switch result view to summary mode
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API endpoint for switching result views returns a list of results in summary mode.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Result data exists
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/results?view=summary with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter view=summary
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of results
5. Verify each result in the array includes fields 'id' and 'name'
6. Verify the total number of results matches the expected limit
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of results in summary mode with correct fields and within expected limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in Switch result view
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all mandatory fields are validated correctly when switching to the result view.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
A meal order has been placed and is in a valid state
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Order History' page
2. Select an active or completed order from the list
3. Tap on the 'Switch Result View' button
4. Verify that all mandatory fields (e.g., 'Customer Name', 'Table Number') are displayed and editable
5. Attempt to submit the form without filling in any mandatory fields
6. Confirm that an error message is displayed for each missing field
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All mandatory fields are correctly validated and display appropriate error messages when left blank.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Calculation accuracy in total bill calculation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the total bill calculation is accurate when switching to the result view.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
A meal order has been placed with known item prices and quantities
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Order History' page
2. Select an active or completed order from the list
3. Tap on the 'Switch Result View' button
4. Verify that the total bill amount is displayed correctly
5. Check if the calculation includes all items and their respective quantities
6. Confirm that any discounts or taxes are accurately applied
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The total bill calculation is accurate and includes all items, discounts, and taxes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Foreign key relationships in order data
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that foreign key relationships are maintained correctly when switching to the result view.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
A meal order has been placed with known customer and table information
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Order History' page
2. Select an active or completed order from the list
3. Tap on the 'Switch Result View' button
4. Verify that the customer name is correctly linked to a valid customer record
5. Check if the table number is associated with a valid table in the system
6. Confirm that any changes made in the result view are reflected in the corresponding database records
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Foreign key relationships are maintained correctly, and changes in the result view are reflected in the database.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Result View Switch
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from network interruptions while switching result views.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a result list
test_steps: 1. User initiates switch to another result view (e.g., clicking on 'Next' button)
2. Simulate network interruption during the switch process
3. Observe if the app shows a loading indicator or error message
4. Wait for the network to resume and verify that the app successfully switches to the next result view
5. Check if all data is correctly displayed in the new result view
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: App should recover from network interruptions and successfully switch to the next result view with correct data display.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Result View Switch
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from crashes while switching result views.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a result list
test_steps: 1. User initiates switch to another result view (e.g., clicking on 'Next' button)
2. Simulate an app crash during the switch process
3. Observe if the app restarts automatically or shows a crash recovery screen
4. Wait for the app to recover and verify that it successfully switches to the next result view
5. Check if all data is correctly displayed in the new result view
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: App should recover from crashes and successfully switch to the next result view with correct data display.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data Persistence Across Crashes During Result View Switch
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app retains data across crashes while switching result views.
preconditions: User is logged in and viewing a result list
test_steps: 1. User initiates switch to another result view (e.g., clicking on 'Next' button)
2. Simulate an app crash during the switch process
3. Observe if the app restarts automatically or shows a crash recovery screen
4. Wait for the app to recover and verify that it successfully switches to the next result view
5. Check if all data is correctly displayed in the new result view
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: App should retain data across crashes and successfully switch to the next result view with correct data display.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under normal load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the performance of selecting an information link in a mobile application under normal user load.
preconditions: Application is installed and logged in with a valid account
No other users are actively using the app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu and tap on 'Information'
Step 3: Scroll through the list of information links until reaching the last item
Step 4: Tap on the last information link and verify that it opens correctly
Step 5: Wait for 10 seconds and observe if any performance issues occur (e.g., lag, crashes)
Step 6: Record the number of successful taps and any errors encountered
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Application should respond within 2 seconds for each tap, with no more than 1 error during the test period.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under high user load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the performance of selecting an information link in a mobile application when multiple users are actively using it.
preconditions: Application is installed and logged in with valid accounts
10 other users are also actively using the app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu and tap on 'Information'
Step 3: Scroll through the list of information links until reaching the last item
Step 4: Tap on the last information link and verify that it opens correctly
Step 5: Wait for 1 minute and observe if any performance issues occur (e.g., lag, crashes)
Step 6: Record the number of successful taps and any errors encountered
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Application should respond within 5 seconds for each tap, with no more than 2 errors during the test period.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under extreme user load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the performance of selecting an information link in a mobile application when the maximum number of users are actively using it.

100 other users are also actively using the app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application
Step 2: Navigate to the main menu and tap on 'Information'
Step 3: Scroll through the list of information links until reaching the last item
Step 4: Tap on the last information link and verify that it opens correctly
Step 5: Wait for 1 minute and observe if any performance issues occur (e.g., lag, crashes)
Step 6: Record the number of successful taps and any errors encountered
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Application should respond within 10 seconds for each tap, with no more than 5 errors during the test period.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection Vulnerability in Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application prevents SQL injection when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Log in to the mobile application with a valid user account.
2. Navigate to the 'Menu' page.
3. Select an item from the menu, such as 'Pizza'.
4. Observe the URL generated for the selected item, e.g., '/menu/item/Pizza'
5. Modify the URL to include a SQL injection payload, e.g., '/menu/item/Pizza%27--'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should redirect to the 'Menu' page without executing any SQL injection payload, and no error messages should be displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application prevents XSS when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Log in to the mobile application with a valid user account.
2. Navigate to the 'Menu' page.
3. Select an item from the menu, such as 'Pizza'.
4. Observe the URL generated for the selected item, e.g., '/menu/item/Pizza'
5. Modify the URL to include a XSS payload, e.g., '/menu/item/<script>alert('XSS')</script>'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should redirect to the 'Menu' page without executing any XSS payload, and no alert boxes should be displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Open Redirect Vulnerability in Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application prevents open redirect when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Log in to the mobile application with a valid user account.
2. Navigate to the 'Menu' page.
3. Select an item from the menu, such as 'Pizza'.
4. Observe the URL generated for the selected item, e.g., '/menu/item/Pizza'
5. Modify the URL to include a redirect payload, e.g., '/menu/item/https://maliciouswebsite.com'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should redirect to the 'Menu' page without following any open redirect payload, and no redirection to malicious websites should occur.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User navigates to information link from main menu
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the user can easily navigate to an information link from the main menu of the mobile application.

Step 2: Scroll through the main menu options until the 'Information' link is visible
Step 3: Tap on the 'Information' link
Step 4: Verify that a new screen opens displaying information about the app or restaurant
Step 5: Confirm that the information displayed is relevant and easy to understand
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user successfully navigates to the information screen with relevant, easily understandable content.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User can access help section from information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the user can easily navigate to a help section from the information link in the mobile application.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User can return to main menu from information link

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a restaurant menu item
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that selecting an information link for a restaurant menu item returns accurate and expected data.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Restaurant menu data is available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurant/menu/<item_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter ?includeDetails=true
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains menu item details including name, description, and price
5. Verify response body includes 'available' boolean indicating if the item is currently available
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Menu item details are returned with correct name, description, price, and availability status.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a restaurant review

Restaurant reviews are available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurant/reviews/<review_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter ?includeComments=true
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains review details including rating, text, and comments
5. Verify response body includes 'helpful' integer indicating the number of helpful votes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Review details are returned with correct rating, text, comments, and helpful vote count.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting the information link for a restaurant reservation

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields when selecting information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all mandatory fields are validated correctly when selecting the information link in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the main menu
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Select Information' link from the main menu
Step 2: Attempt to submit the form without entering any data in mandatory fields
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed for each mandatory field indicating it is required
Step 4: Enter valid data into all mandatory fields and attempt to submit the form again
Step 5: Verify that the form submits successfully and no error messages are displayed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display an error message for each mandatory field when left empty, and allow submission only when all mandatory fields are filled with valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of data type constraints for numerical input when selecting information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application correctly validates data type constraints for numerical inputs when selecting the information link.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the main menu
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Select Information' link from the main menu
Step 2: Enter a non-numeric value in a field that requires numerical input (e.g., age)
Step 3: Attempt to submit the form
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that only numeric values are allowed
Step 5: Enter a valid numeric value and attempt to submit the form again
Step 6: Verify that the form submits successfully without any errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display an error message when a non-numeric value is entered in a field requiring numerical input, and allow submission only when a valid numeric value is provided.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of transaction integrity for CRUD operations on selected information
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application maintains transaction integrity during CRUD operations when selecting information.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the main menu
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Select Information' link from the main menu
Step 2: Perform a CREATE operation by adding new information and verify that it appears in the list
Step 3: Perform an UPDATE operation by modifying existing information and verify that the changes are reflected in the list
Step 4: Perform a DELETE operation by removing information and verify that it is no longer visible in the list
Step 5: Verify that all CRUD operations are performed successfully without any data loss or corruption
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All CRUD operations should be performed successfully, and the application should maintain transaction integrity without any data loss or corruption.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during information link selection

User is on a stable internet connection
test_steps: 1. Open the restaurant app and navigate to the 'Menu' page
2. Tap on the 'Select Information Link' button
3. Introduce a network interruption (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi or switch to airplane mode)
4. Wait for the network to be restored
5. Verify that the app automatically reconnects and allows the user to select an information link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app successfully recovers from network interruption and allows the user to continue selecting an information link.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after selecting an information link
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the mobile app can recover from crashes while selecting an information link.

User has selected an information link
test_steps: 1. Open the restaurant app and navigate to the 'Menu' page
2. Tap on the 'Select Information Link' button
3. Select a specific information link (e.g., opening a web URL)
4. Introduce an app crash (e.g., force close the app from the device's task manager)
5. Restart the app and verify that it opens to the last selected information link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app successfully recovers from a crash and returns to the last selected information link.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data persistence across crashes while selecting an information link
