User has selected an information link
test_steps: 1. Open the restaurant app and navigate to the 'Menu' page
2. Tap on the 'Select Information Link' button
3. Select a specific information link (e.g., opening a web URL)
4. Introduce an app crash (e.g., force close the app from the device's task manager)
5. Restart the app and verify that the selected information link is still available in the 'History' section
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app persists the selected information link across crashes and retains it in the 'History' section for at least 24 hours.

test_data.expected_limit: Selected information link should be saved for at least 24 hours

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under normal load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can handle a moderate number of users selecting an information link simultaneously without performance degradation.
preconditions: Application is deployed on a production-like environment
Initial user base is 1000 users
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 users logging into the application
Step 2: Each user selects an information link within the app
Step 3: Monitor the response time for each user's selection
Step 4: Verify that no more than 5% of selections have a response time greater than 1 second
Step 5: Confirm that all users are able to select the information link without encountering errors or crashes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: 95% of selections should complete within 1 second, with no more than 5 users experiencing delays.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under peak load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can handle a high number of users selecting an information link simultaneously without performance degradation.

Initial user base is 5000 users
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 2500 users logging into the application
Step 2: Each user selects an information link within the app
Step 3: Monitor the response time for each user's selection
Step 4: Verify that no more than 10% of selections have a response time greater than 2 seconds
Step 5: Confirm that all users are able to select the information link without encountering errors or crashes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: 90% of selections should complete within 2 seconds, with no more than 10 users experiencing delays.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Selecting information link under stress load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can handle an extremely high number of users selecting an information link simultaneously without performance degradation.

test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 5000 users logging into the application
Step 2: Each user selects an information link within the app
Step 3: Monitor the response time for each user's selection
Step 4: Verify that no more than 20% of selections have a response time greater than 5 seconds
Step 5: Confirm that all users are able to select the information link without encountering errors or crashes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: 80% of selections should complete within 5 seconds, with no more than 20 users experiencing delays.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection Attack on Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application prevents SQL injection when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid account
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'My Orders' page.
2. Enter a malicious SQL query into the order ID input field, e.g., '1; DROP TABLE orders--'.
3. Click on the 'Select Order' button.
4. Verify that no data is displayed and an error message indicating SQL injection attempt is shown.
5. Confirm that the database remains intact and no tables are dropped.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent SQL injection, display an error message, and maintain the integrity of the database.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) on Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application prevents XSS when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid account
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'My Orders' page.
2. Enter a malicious script into the order ID input field, e.g., '<script>alert('XSS')</script>'.
3. Click on the 'Select Order' button.
4. Verify that no data is displayed and an alert box with the message 'XSS' does not appear.
5. Confirm that the application sanitizes the input and prevents execution of malicious scripts.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent XSS, display an error message, and maintain user session integrity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Authentication Bypass on Information Link Selection
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application prevents authentication bypass when selecting information links.
preconditions: User is logged out
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'My Orders' page.
2. Attempt to select an order without being authenticated by entering a valid user ID and password.
3. Verify that the application redirects to the login page or displays an error message indicating authentication failure.
4. Confirm that the user is not able to access the selected order information.
5. Log in with a different account and verify that the selected order information can be accessed successfully.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent authentication bypass, redirect to login page or display error message, and maintain user session integrity.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify information link is clearly labeled and easy to find on the main screen

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test the time taken to navigate from main screen to information page

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Data Persistence
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can persist data across network interruptions.
preconditions: App is running with a valid session
User has selected an information link
test_steps: 1. User selects an information link and inputs details (e.g., 'Dining at XYZ', '2023-10-01')
2. App sends the data to the server and receives a confirmation response

4. User attempts to submit the information link again
5. Verify that the app persists the input details locally and shows a message indicating offline submission
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should persist the input details locally and allow resubmission upon network reconnection.

User has selected an information link

4. Reopen the app and verify that the input details are still present in the form
5. Submit the information link again
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from crashes and persist the input details, allowing the user to complete the submission.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling After Data Persistence

User has selected an information link

5. Submit the information link again

preconditions: Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+
WiFi network available
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+.
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page.
Step 3: Enter a price range, e.g., '$10 - $50'.
Step 4: Tap the search button.
Step 5: Verify that results are displayed within 2 seconds and include restaurants within the specified price range. Ensure UI elements are legible and functional.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display within 2 seconds, showing restaurants in the '$10 - $50' price range with a legible and functional UI.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Valid Price Range
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results when a valid price range is provided.
preconditions: API endpoint /api/search/price is accessible
Valid JWT token for authentication
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/price with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameters: min_price=10&max_price=50
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants within the price range [10, 50]
5. Verify each restaurant in the response has a 'price' field within the specified range
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of restaurants with prices between $10 and $50, inclusive.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Empty Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns an empty array when no restaurants match the specified price range.
preconditions: API endpoint /api/search/price is accessible
Valid JWT token for authentication
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/price with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameters: min_price=100&max_price=200
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body is an empty array
5. Verify the 'message' field in the response indicates no results found
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an empty array and a message indicating no restaurants were found within the specified price range.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Invalid Price Range
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns an error when an invalid price range is provided.
preconditions: API endpoint /api/search/price is accessible
Valid JWT token for authentication
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/price with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameters: min_price=50&max_price=10
3. Verify HTTP 400 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an error message indicating invalid price range
5. Verify the 'error_code' field in the response is 'INVALID_PRICE_RANGE'
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a 400 status code and an error message indicating the price range is invalid.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Valid Price Range
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates that the search functionality returns results within a specified price range.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a minimum price of $10 and a maximum price of $50 in the search filters.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that all displayed restaurants have prices between $10 and $50 inclusive.
5. Check that no restaurant with a price outside this range is shown.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All displayed restaurants have prices between $10 and $50 inclusive.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Mandatory Fields Validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates that the search functionality requires both minimum and maximum price fields to be filled.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Leave the minimum price field empty and enter a maximum price of $50.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that an error message appears indicating that both fields are required.
5. Fill in the minimum price field with $10 and click on the 'Search' button again.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that both fields are required when only one is filled.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by price - Data Type Constraints
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates that the search functionality accepts only numeric values for the minimum and maximum price fields.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a non-numeric value (e.g., 'abc') in the minimum price field and $50 in the maximum price field.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that an error message appears indicating that only numeric values are allowed.
5. Clear the minimum price field, enter a valid number (e.g., $10), and click on the 'Search' button again.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that only numeric values are allowed when a non-numeric value is entered.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search by Price
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from network interruptions during a search by price operation.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a price range (e.g., $10 - $50) into the search criteria field.
3. Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search operation.
4. Introduce a network interruption during the search process (e.g., toggle airplane mode or disconnect Wi-Fi).
5. Observe if the app handles the network interruption gracefully and resumes the search operation once the connection is restored.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a message indicating that the search has been resumed upon reconnection, and it should continue to show results for the specified price range.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search by Price
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from crashes during a search by price operation.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile application and navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a price range (e.g., $10 - $50) into the search criteria field.
3. Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search operation.
4. Simulate an app crash during the search process (e.g., force stop the app from the device's task manager).
5. Reopen the application and observe if it recovers the previous state, including the search criteria and results.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from the crash and display the search results for the specified price range, maintaining all user inputs.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Search by Price

GPS signal is available

2. Enter a price range (e.g., $10 - $50) into the search criteria field.
3. Tap the 'Search' button to initiate the search operation.

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a message indicating that the session has timed out and prompt the user to log in again, allowing them to continue their search operation.

Step 4: Check if any error messages or unexpected behavior occurs
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not crash or allow unauthorized access
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search functionality should reject malicious SQL queries and prevent any unauthorized access.

test_data.expected_result: No results returned and no errors or crashes observed

Step 3: Verify that no results are returned
Step 4: Check if any error messages or unexpected behavior occurs
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not execute the script and prevents any unauthorized access
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search functionality should reject malicious scripts and prevent XSS attacks.

test_data.expected_result: No results returned and no errors or crashes observed, script execution prevented

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Valid Search Query
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a valid search query returns the expected results.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?query=New York
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of destinations
5. Verify the first destination in the array has the name 'New York'
6. Verify the number of results is within the expected limit (e.g., maximum 10 results)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response contains an array of destinations with 'New York' as the first result and a total count within the limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Invalid Search Query
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that an invalid search query returns an empty result set.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?query=XYZ
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an empty array of destinations
5. Verify the number of results is 0
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response contains an empty array of destinations with a total count of 0.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Rate Limiting
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API rate limiting is enforced for search requests.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send 20 GET requests to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?query=New York for all requests
3. Verify the first 15 requests return HTTP 200 status code
4. Verify the next 5 requests return HTTP 429 status code (Too Many Requests)
5. Wait for 1 minute and send another GET request with query parameter: ?query=New York
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: First 15 requests return HTTP 200 status code, next 5 requests return HTTP 429 status code, and a subsequent request after waiting returns HTTP 200 status code.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Mandatory field validation

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Transaction integrity

User is logged in

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search by Destination

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search by Destination

phase2_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality across various device screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: Install the mobile app on a large tablet (e.g., Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+) and a small smartphone (e.g., Google Pixel 3a)
test_steps: Step 1: On the large tablet, open the app and navigate to the 'Search' tab
Step 2: Tap on the search bar and enter 'Paris'
Step 3: Verify that the search results display restaurants in Paris
Step 4: Check if the search results are displayed in a readable format with all necessary information visible
Step 5: On the small smartphone, repeat steps 1-4 to ensure consistency
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants in Paris and are displayed in a readable format on both devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination with different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G)
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate search functionality across different network types.
preconditions: Install the mobile app on an iPhone 12 and a Pixel 5
Ensure both devices are connected to WiFi, 4G, and 5G networks
test_steps: Step 1: On the iOS device, open the app and navigate to the 'Search' tab
Step 2: Tap on the search bar and enter 'London'
Step 3: Verify that the search results display restaurants in London
Step 4: Check if the search results are displayed within 5 seconds on all network types
Step 5: On the Android device, repeat steps 1-4 to ensure consistency
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display restaurants in London and are displayed within 5 seconds on all network types.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Valid Search Query
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a valid search query returns the correct results.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?query=New York
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of destinations
5. Verify the first destination in the array has a name 'New York'
6. Verify the number of results is within the limit of 10
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response contains an array of destinations with at least one entry named 'New York' and a total count not exceeding 10.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Invalid Search Query
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that an invalid search query returns an empty result set.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?query=XYZ
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an empty array of destinations
5. Verify the number of results is exactly 0
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response contains an empty array of destinations with a total count of 0.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Rate Limiting
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API handles rate limiting correctly.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> for 50 times in 60 seconds
2. Verify HTTP 429 status code returned after the first 50 requests
3. Wait for 60 seconds
4. Send GET request to /api/search/destination with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> for another 10 times
5. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned for all 10 requests
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns HTTP 429 after the first 50 requests and HTTP 200 for subsequent requests after a 60-second wait.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Mandatory field validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality requires a valid destination name to be entered.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Search page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page
2. Click on the 'Destination' input field
3. Leave the field empty and click on the search button
4. Verify that an error message appears indicating a required field
5. Enter a valid destination name (e.g., 'New York') and click on the search button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that the 'Destination' field is mandatory when left empty.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Data type constraints
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality accepts only string values for the destination name.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Search page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page
2. Click on the 'Destination' input field
3. Enter a numeric value (e.g., '123') and click on the search button
4. Verify that an error message appears indicating invalid data type
5. Enter a valid destination name (e.g., 'New York') and click on the search button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that the 'Destination' field only accepts string values.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by destination - Transaction integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search functionality maintains transaction integrity when performing multiple searches.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the Search page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page
2. Click on the 'Destination' input field and enter a valid destination name (e.g., 'New York')
3. Click on the search button and verify that results are displayed
4. Click on the 'Destination' input field again and enter another valid destination name (e.g., 'Los Angeles')
5. Click on the search button and verify that results for the new destination are displayed without any data corruption
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Results for both searches are displayed correctly without any data corruption or loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search by Destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from network interruptions during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start the app and navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a destination name (e.g., 'New York') into the search bar.
3. Tap on the search button.
4. Simulate network interruption by turning off the device's internet connection.
5. Observe if the app displays an error message or continues to load.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a clear error message indicating no internet connection and allow the user to retry the search.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search by Destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from crashes during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start the app and navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a destination name (e.g., 'New York') into the search bar.
3. Tap on the search button.
4. Simulate an app crash by force quitting the app from the device's task manager.
5. Restart the app and navigate to the 'Search' page.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover gracefully, displaying the search bar with the previously entered destination name.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Search by Destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app handles session timeouts during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start the app and navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a destination name (e.g., 'New York') into the search bar.
3. Tap on the search button.
4. Wait for the session timeout period (e.g., 30 minutes).
5. Observe if the app prompts for login or redirects to the login screen.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should prompt the user to log in again after the session timeout period, allowing them to continue their search.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a range of prices and destinations
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle concurrent price and destination searches without performance degradation.
preconditions: System is up and running
Multiple users are logged in
test_steps: Step 1: User A initiates a search for restaurants within $50 and within 10 miles of 'New York City'
Step 2: User B simultaneously searches for restaurants within $100 and within 5 miles of 'Los Angeles'
Step 3: User C performs a search for restaurants within $75 and within 20 miles of 'Chicago'
Step 4: Monitor the system's response time for each user
Step 5: Verify that all searches complete within 5 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All searches complete within 5 seconds, with a maximum of 200 results per search

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Concurrent searches for multiple destinations and price ranges
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the system can handle a large number of concurrent searches for different destinations and price ranges.
preconditions: System is up and running
100 users are logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Generate 50 unique search requests with random price ranges between $30-$200
Step 2: Distribute these searches evenly across 100 users
Step 3: Monitor the system's response time for each search request
Step 4: Verify that all searches complete within 10 seconds
Step 5: Check that the total number of results returned is at least 20,000
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All searches complete within 10 seconds, with a minimum of 20,000 results returned

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search performance under high load
phase2_test_cases.description: Test the system's ability to handle a large number of concurrent searches and ensure it remains responsive.
preconditions: System is up and running
1,000 users are logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Generate 50 unique search requests with random price ranges between $20-$300
Step 2: Distribute these searches evenly across 1,000 users
Step 3: Monitor the system's response time for each search request
Step 4: Verify that all searches complete within 15 seconds
Step 5: Check that the total number of results returned is at least 200,000
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All searches complete within 15 seconds, with a minimum of 200,000 results returned

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Input validation for price range in search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the system rejects invalid price ranges and accepts valid ones.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant food service platform.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page.
Step 2: Enter a minimum price of '$0.01' in the 'Price Range' field.
Step 3: Enter a maximum price of '$1000.00' in the 'Price Range' field.
Step 4: Click on the 'Search' button.
Step 5: Verify that the search results are displayed with prices between $0.01 and $1000.00.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display only items within the specified price range of $0.01 to $1000.00.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of destination search input
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensures that the system rejects invalid or malicious destination inputs and accepts valid ones.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant food service platform.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page.
Step 2: Enter a valid destination, e.g., 'New York City'.
Step 3: Click on the 'Search' button.
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed for New York City.
Step 5: Enter an invalid destination input, e.g., '<script>alert('XSS')</script>'.
Step 6: Click on the 'Search' button.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Valid destination search returns results for New York City, while invalid input does not cause any security vulnerabilities.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Input validation for price and destination search with special characters
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the system handles special characters in price and destination inputs correctly.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant food service platform.
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Search' page.
Step 2: Enter a price range with special characters, e.g., '$10.99 - $50.00'.
Step 3: Click on the 'Search' button.
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed within the specified price range of $10.99 to $50.00.
Step 5: Enter a destination with special characters, e.g., 'San Francisco, CA'.
Step 6: Click on the 'Search' button.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display only items within the specified price range and for the specified destination with special characters.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for a restaurant within a price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can search for restaurants by specifying a price range and destination, and receive relevant results.
preconditions: User is on the home page of the food service application
test_steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Search' button located in the top navigation bar
Step 2: Enter a valid price range (e.g., '$5-$10') into the 'Price Range' input field
Step 3: Select a valid destination from the dropdown menu (e.g., 'New York City')
Step 4: Click on the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 5: Verify that the search results display restaurants within the specified price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results should display at least one restaurant within the $5-$10 price range in New York City, with clear and relevant information for each result.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Error handling when entering invalid price range
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application displays an error message when users enter an invalid price range.
preconditions: User is on the home page of the food service application
test_steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Search' button located in the top navigation bar
Step 2: Enter an invalid price range (e.g., '$50-$10') into the 'Price Range' input field
Step 3: Select a valid destination from the dropdown menu (e.g., 'New York City')
Step 4: Click on the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the price range is invalid
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed stating 'Invalid price range. Please enter a valid range.'

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Navigation to search results from home page
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can navigate directly to the search results page from the home page by clicking on the 'Search' button.
preconditions: User is on the home page of the food service application
test_steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Search' button located in the top navigation bar
Step 2: Enter a valid price range (e.g., '$5-$10') into the 'Price Range' input field
Step 3: Select a valid destination from the dropdown menu (e.g., 'New York City')
Step 4: Click on the 'Search' button to initiate the search
Step 5: Verify that the user is redirected to the search results page displaying restaurants within the specified price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user should be redirected to a new page with the title 'Search Results' and display restaurants matching the search criteria.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on iOS device
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the price and destination search function correctly on an iOS device.
preconditions: User has installed the app
Device is running iOS 14.5 or later
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the app on an iPhone 12 Pro Max
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' tab
Step 3: Enter a price range (e.g., $10 - $50)
Step 4: Select a destination city (e.g., San Francisco)
Step 5: Click on the search button and verify that results are displayed within 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display within 2 seconds and include at least 10 restaurants within the specified price range and destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on Android device with different screen size
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the price and destination search function correctly on an Android device with a different screen size.
preconditions: User has installed the app
Device is running Android 10 or later
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the app on a Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' tab
Step 3: Enter a price range (e.g., $5 - $20)
Step 4: Select a destination city (e.g., New York City)
Step 5: Click on the search button and verify that results are displayed within 3 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display within 3 seconds and include at least 15 restaurants within the specified price range and destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on web browser with different network types
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the price and destination search function correctly on a web browser with different network types.
preconditions: User has access to a web browser
Device is connected to WiFi
test_steps: Step 1: Open Chrome on an Android device
Step 2: Navigate to the restaurant app's website
Step 3: Enter a price range (e.g., $30 - $70)
Step 4: Select a destination city (e.g., Los Angeles)
Step 5: Click on the search button and verify that results are displayed within 2.5 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display within 2.5 seconds and include at least 12 restaurants within the specified price range and destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results when searching for restaurants based on price and destination.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search with query parameters: price=50-100, destination='New York'
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify each restaurant in the array has a price between $50 and $100
6. Verify each restaurant in the array has 'New York' as its destination
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of 10 restaurants within the specified price range and destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with a specific cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results when searching for restaurants based on cuisine type.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search with query parameter: cuisine='Italian'
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify each restaurant in the array has 'Italian' as its cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of 5 Italian restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with a specific rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results when searching for restaurants based on rating.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants/search with query parameter: rating=4.5
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify each restaurant in the array has a rating of at least 4.5
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of 3 restaurants with a rating of at least 4.5.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of price input in search functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system accepts only valid numeric values for price and rejects non-numeric inputs.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant/food service application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a valid numeric value in the 'Price' field, e.g., '50'.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that the search results are displayed with items priced at or below 50.
5. Clear the 'Price' field and enter an invalid non-numeric value, e.g., 'abc'.
6. Click on the 'Search' button.
7. Verify that no search results are displayed and an error message is shown indicating invalid input.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Valid numeric price should return relevant search results, while non-numeric input should display an error message.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of destination search functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system accepts only valid destination names and rejects empty or invalid inputs.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant/food service application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a valid destination name in the 'Destination' field, e.g., 'New York'.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that the search results are displayed with items available in New York.
5. Clear the 'Destination' field and leave it empty.
6. Click on the 'Search' button.
7. Verify that no search results are displayed and an error message is shown indicating invalid input.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Valid destination name should return relevant search results, while empty or invalid input should display an error message.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in search functionality
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system requires both price and destination fields to be filled before performing a search.
preconditions: User is logged into the restaurant/food service application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Leave the 'Price' field empty and enter a valid destination name, e.g., 'New York'.
3. Click on the 'Search' button.
4. Verify that no search results are displayed and an error message is shown indicating missing price input.
5. Clear the 'Destination' field and leave it empty.
6. Enter a valid numeric value in the 'Price' field, e.g., '50'.
7. Click on the 'Search' button.
8. Verify that no search results are displayed and an error message is shown indicating missing destination input.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Both price and destination fields must be filled before performing a search, otherwise an error message should be displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Price and Destination Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application can recover from network interruptions during price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search for a specific restaurant
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a price and destination search for a specific restaurant (e.g., 'Italian cuisine', 'New York City')
Step 2: Simulate network interruption during the search process
Step 3: Wait for the network to recover
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed upon network recovery
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not crash and continues to function normally
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed within 10 seconds after network recovery, without any crashes or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Price and Destination Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application can recover from crashes during price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search for a specific restaurant
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a price and destination search for a specific restaurant (e.g., 'Japanese cuisine', 'San Francisco')
Step 2: Simulate an app crash during the search process
Step 3: Restart the application
Step 4: Verify that the search results are displayed upon restart
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not crash and continues to function normally
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed within 10 seconds after app restart, without any crashes or errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data Persistence Across Crashes During Price and Destination Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application persists search data across crashes during price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
Search for a specific restaurant
test_steps: Step 1: Initiate a price and destination search for a specific restaurant (e.g., 'Mexican cuisine', 'Los Angeles')
Step 2: Simulate an app crash during the search process
Step 3: Restart the application
Step 4: Verify that the search criteria are still present in the search input fields
Step 5: Confirm that the search results are displayed upon restart
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search criteria and results are preserved across app crashes, allowing the user to resume their search without losing progress.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a range of prices and destinations
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle concurrent price and destination searches without performance degradation.
preconditions: System is up and running
Multiple users are logged in
test_steps: Step 1: User A initiates a search for restaurants with a price range of $50-$100 and destination 'New York City'
Step 2: User B simultaneously initiates a search for restaurants with a price range of $150-$200 and destination 'Los Angeles'

Step 5: Verify that all searches complete within 5 seconds
Step 6: Check that no errors or timeouts occur during the search process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All searches complete within 5 seconds with no errors or timeouts, demonstrating system scalability and performance under concurrent load.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Concurrent search for multiple destinations with varying price ranges
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the system can handle simultaneous searches for different destinations with various price ranges without performance issues.
preconditions: System is up and running
Multiple users are logged in

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with a large price range and multiple destinations
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's performance when handling a single search request with a wide price range and multiple destination options.

Multiple users are logged in

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search completes within 10 seconds with no errors or timeouts, and includes restaurants from all specified destinations and price ranges, demonstrating system scalability and performance under a single large request.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Input validation for price search field
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects non-numeric input in the price search field.

Step 4: Submit the search form

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Input validation for destination search field

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Input validation for price and destination search fields simultaneously
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects non-numeric input in the price field and input exceeding the maximum length in the destination field when both are filled.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User can search for restaurants by price and destination on desktop
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can input valid price ranges and select a destination to search for restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page should display at least one restaurant with a price between $50 and $100 in New York City.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User can search for restaurants by price and destination on mobile
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that users can input valid price ranges and select a destination to search for restaurants on a mobile device.
preconditions: Open the restaurant app on a mobile device
test_steps: 1. Tap on the 'Search' icon located in the bottom navigation bar.
2. Enter a maximum price of $100 in the 'Price Range' field.
3. Select 'Los Angeles' from the 'Destination' dropdown menu.
4. Tap on the 'Submit' button to perform the search.
5. Verify that the search results page displays restaurants within the specified price range and destination.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results page should display at least one restaurant with a price between $50 and $100 in Los Angeles.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Error message is displayed when invalid price range is entered
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an error message is displayed when users enter an invalid price range.
preconditions: Open the restaurant app on a desktop browser
test_steps: 1. Click on the 'Search' button located in the top navigation bar.
2. Enter a minimum price of $500 and a maximum price of $100 in the 'Price Range' field.
3. Select 'Chicago' from the 'Destination' dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Submit' button to perform the search.
5. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the price range is invalid.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed stating 'Invalid price range. Please enter a valid range.'

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on iOS device
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate price and destination search functionality on an iOS device.
preconditions: User has installed the app
Device is running iOS 15
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app on an iPhone 12 Pro Max with iOS 15
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' tab at the bottom of the screen
Step 3: Enter a destination address in the search bar (e.g., 'New York City, NY')
Step 4: Select the destination from the suggestions list
Step 5: Enter a price range (e.g., '$10 - $20 per person')
Step 6: Tap on the 'Search' button
Step 7: Verify that search results are displayed within 3 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed within 3 seconds and contain at least 5 restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on Android device

Step 6: Tap on the 'Search' button
Step 7: Verify that search results are displayed within 3 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed within 3 seconds and contain at least 5 restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Price and destination search on different screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate price and destination search functionality on devices with different screen sizes.
preconditions: User has installed the app
Devices are running iOS 15 and Android 12
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app on an iPhone SE (iOS 15)
Step 2: Tap on the 'Search' tab at the bottom of the screen
Step 3: Enter a destination address in the search bar (e.g., 'Chicago, IL')
Step 4: Select the destination from the suggestions list

Step 6: Tap on the 'Search' button
Step 7: Verify that search results are displayed within 3 seconds and fit well on the screen

Step 9: Repeat steps 2-6
Step 10: Verify that search results are displayed within 3 seconds and fit well on the screen
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed within 3 seconds and fit well on both iPhone SE and Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+ screens.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants within a price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results based on specified price range and destination.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameters 'price_min=50' and 'destination=New York'
Step 2: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains restaurants within the specified price range (min $50)
Step 5: Verify response body includes restaurants located in New York
Step 6: Verify that the number of results is less than or equal to 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of restaurants within $50 price range and located in New York with less than or equal to 100 results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants with invalid price range and destination
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns an error response when invalid price range or destination is provided.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameters 'price_min=-50' and 'destination=InvalidCity'
Step 2: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Verify HTTP 400 status code returned
Step 4: Verify response body contains error message indicating invalid price range or destination
Step 5: Verify that no restaurants are returned in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a 400 Bad Request status code with an error message for invalid price range or destination.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for restaurants and verify response time
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns results within a specified response time.
preconditions: API endpoint is accessible
Valid authentication token available
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameters 'price_min=50' and 'destination=New York'
Step 2: Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 3: Measure the time taken for API response
Step 4: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 5: Verify that the response time is less than or equal to 2 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns results within a maximum of 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of price and destination search input fields
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the price and destination search fields accept valid data types, ranges, and formats.
preconditions: User is logged in to the restaurant/food service application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter a price range of '$50 - $100' into the price search field.
3. Select 'New York City' from the destination dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Search' button.
5. Verify that the search results are displayed within 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The search results should display restaurants within the specified price range and destination, with a response time of less than 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in price and destination search
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the price and destination fields are mandatory and cannot be left empty.

test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Leave the price search field empty.
3. Select 'New York City' from the destination dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Search' button.
5. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the price field cannot be left blank.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that the price field is mandatory and cannot be left empty.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of data type constraints in price and destination search

test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Search' page.
2. Enter 'abc' into the price search field.
3. Select 'New York City' from the destination dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Search' button.
5. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the price must be a numeric value.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message should be displayed indicating that the price field must accept only numeric values.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during price and destination search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application can recover from network interruptions during a price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start a price and destination search with actual values (e.g., 'New York' as destination, '$50' as budget)
2. Introduce a network interruption during the search process
3. Wait for the network to resume
4. Verify that the search results are displayed without any errors
5. Confirm that the application does not crash and remains responsive
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed successfully after network interruption, no crashes observed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery during price and destination search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application can recover from crashes during a price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start a price and destination search with actual values (e.g., 'Los Angeles' as destination, '$75' as budget)
2. Introduce an intentional crash during the search process
3. Restart the application
4. Verify that the search results are displayed without any errors
5. Confirm that the application does not crash and remains responsive
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed successfully after app restart, no crashes observed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data persistence across crashes during price and destination search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application persists data across crashes during a price and destination search.
preconditions: User is logged in
GPS signal is available
test_steps: 1. Start a price and destination search with actual values (e.g., 'Chicago' as destination, '$100' as budget)
2. Introduce an intentional crash during the search process
3. Restart the application
4. Verify that the search parameters are retained across crashes
5. Confirm that the application does not crash and remains responsive
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search parameters (destination, budget) are retained across crashes, no data loss observed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - High load test
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle a high number of concurrent search requests for different restaurant types.
preconditions: System is running with default configuration
Initial data load includes at least 10,000 restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Start generating 500 concurrent search requests for 'Italian' restaurants
Step 2: Monitor the system's response time and throughput during the test
Step 3: Verify that all search requests receive a successful HTTP 200 status code within 2 seconds
Step 4: Check that the search results are accurate and relevant to the query
Step 5: Ensure that no errors or crashes occur during the test
Step 6: Analyze system metrics (CPU, memory, network) to ensure they remain stable
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle up to 500 concurrent search requests for 'Italian' restaurants within 2 seconds, with a throughput of at least 400 requests per minute.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - Stress test
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle an extremely high number of concurrent search requests for a single restaurant type.
preconditions: System is running with default configuration
Initial data load includes at least 10,000 restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Start generating 2,000 concurrent search requests for 'Chinese' restaurants
Step 2: Monitor the system's response time and throughput during the test
Step 3: Verify that all search requests receive a successful HTTP 200 status code within 5 seconds
Step 4: Check that the search results are accurate and relevant to the query
Step 5: Ensure that no errors or crashes occur during the test
Step 6: Analyze system metrics (CPU, memory, network) to ensure they remain stable
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle up to 2,000 concurrent search requests for 'Chinese' restaurants within 5 seconds, with a throughput of at least 1,000 requests per minute.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - Load test with varying search terms
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle a range of different search terms for restaurant types.
preconditions: System is running with default configuration
Initial data load includes at least 10,000 restaurants
test_steps: Step 1: Start generating 500 concurrent search requests with varying terms (e.g., 'Italian', 'Chinese', 'Mexican')
Step 2: Monitor the system's response time and throughput during the test
Step 3: Verify that all search requests receive a successful HTTP 200 status code within 3 seconds
Step 4: Check that the search results are accurate and relevant to the query
Step 5: Ensure that no errors or crashes occur during the test
Step 6: Analyze system metrics (CPU, memory, network) to ensure they remain stable
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle up to 500 concurrent search requests with varying terms for restaurant types within 3 seconds, with a throughput of at least 300 requests per minute.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection of SQL Queries in Search Input
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application does not allow SQL injection attacks when searching by restaurant type.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page and enter a SQL injection payload into the 'Restaurant Type' search field. Payload: ' OR '1'='1'
Step 2: Submit the search query.
Step 3: Verify that no results are returned or an error message is displayed instead of exposing sensitive data.
Step 4: Check the application logs for any SQL injection attempts.
Step 5: Confirm that the database remains intact and no unauthorized access occurs.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent SQL injection attacks and not return any sensitive data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Search Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application does not allow XSS attacks when displaying search results.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page and enter an XSS payload into the 'Restaurant Type' search field. Payload: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
Step 2: Submit the search query.
Step 3: Verify that no script is executed on the client side.
Step 4: Check the application logs for any suspicious activity related to XSS.
Step 5: Confirm that the search results are displayed correctly without executing any malicious scripts.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent XSS attacks and not execute any malicious scripts in the search results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Authentication Bypass via SQL Injection
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application does not allow authentication bypass through SQL injection.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the login page and enter a username and password with an SQL injection payload. Username: 'admin' OR '1'='1', Password: any
Step 2: Attempt to log in.
Step 3: Verify that the login fails and no unauthorized access occurs.
Step 4: Check the application logs for any authentication attempts with SQL injection payloads.
Step 5: Confirm that the user remains logged out and is not granted access.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should prevent authentication bypass through SQL injection attacks.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with valid token
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user can search for restaurants by type using a valid authentication token.
preconditions: User is authenticated and has a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants?type=Italian with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
3. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
4. Verify each restaurant in the array has a 'type' field set to 'Italian'
5. Verify that the number of results is within the expected limit (e.g., maximum 100 results)
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a list of Italian restaurants with HTTP 200 status code and within the expected limit.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with invalid token
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user cannot search for restaurants by type using an invalid authentication token.
preconditions: User is not authenticated or has an invalid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants?type=Italian with header Authorization: Bearer <invalid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 401 status code returned
3. Verify response body contains error message indicating unauthorized access
4. Verify that no restaurants are returned in the response body
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an HTTP 401 status code and error message indicating unauthorized access when using an invalid token.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type with no results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user receives an empty list when searching for restaurants of a type that does not exist.
preconditions: User is authenticated and has a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants?type=NonExistentType with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
3. Verify response body is an empty array
4. Verify that the number of results is 0
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User receives an empty list of restaurants with HTTP 200 status code when searching for a non-existent type.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - Mandatory field validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the 'restaurant_type' field is mandatory and cannot be left empty.

Application is open
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the search page.
2. Click on the 'Search by restaurant type' option.
3. Leave the 'restaurant_type' field empty and click 'Search'.
4. Verify that an error message appears indicating that the 'restaurant_type' field is required.
5. Enter a valid restaurant type in the 'restaurant_type' field and click 'Search'.
6. Verify that the search results are displayed correctly.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that the 'restaurant_type' field is required when left empty.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - Data type constraints

Application is open

6. Verify that the search results are displayed correctly.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message appears indicating that the input is invalid when a non-string value is entered.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by restaurant type - Transaction integrity
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the search operation is atomic and does not leave partial data in the system.
preconditions: User is logged in
Application is open
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the search page.
2. Click on the 'Search by restaurant type' option.
3. Enter a valid restaurant type in the 'restaurant_type' field and click 'Search'.
4. Verify that the search results are displayed correctly without any partial data.
5. Check the database for consistency and ensure no partial records were created.
6. Repeat the search with different restaurant types to verify transaction integrity.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results are displayed correctly without any partial data, and database remains consistent after each search operation.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by specific dish - Valid Search Term
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns correct results when a valid search term is provided.
preconditions: The mobile application is running
User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/dish with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?searchTerm=Spaghetti
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of dishes
5. Verify the first dish in the array has a name field with value 'Spaghetti'
6. Verify the number of results is less than or equal to 10
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of dishes with at least one dish named 'Spaghetti' and a total count less than or equal to 10.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by specific dish - Invalid Search Term
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API returns an empty array when an invalid search term is provided.
preconditions: The mobile application is running
User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/dish with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?searchTerm=XYZ
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body is an empty array
5. Verify the number of results is exactly 0
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an empty array when an invalid search term 'XYZ' is provided.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by specific dish - Rate Limiting
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API handles rate limiting correctly when multiple requests are made within a short period.
preconditions: The mobile application is running
User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send 5 GET requests to /api/search/dish with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> and query parameter ?searchTerm=Spaghetti
2. Wait for 1 second between each request
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned for all requests
4. Verify response body contains an array of dishes for each request
5. Verify the number of results is less than or equal to 10 for each request
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an array of dishes with at least one dish named 'Spaghetti' and a total count less than or equal to 10 for each of the 5 requests.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search by Specific Dish - Valid Input

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the application handles network interruptions gracefully during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
Network connection is stable
test_steps: 1. Start a search for a specific dish, e.g., 'Pizza'
2. Introduce a network interruption (e.g., toggle airplane mode)
3. Observe if the app displays an error message or retry option
4. Re-establish network connection and observe if the search results are loaded successfully
5. Verify that the search history is preserved across network interruptions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display an error message and retry option upon network interruption, and recover successfully when the network is re-established. Search history should be preserved.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Search Operation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the application recovers from crashes during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
Perform a search for a specific dish
test_steps: 1. Start a search for a specific dish, e.g., 'Burger'
2. Introduce an app crash (e.g., force close the app)
3. Reopen the app and navigate to the search page
4. Observe if the previous search query is still visible in the search bar
5. Verify that the search results are loaded successfully upon reopening
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from crashes and display the previous search query in the search bar. Search results should be loaded successfully upon reopening.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior During Search
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the application handles GPS signal loss during a search operation.
preconditions: User is logged in and on the search page
Perform a search for a specific dish
test_steps: 1. Start a search for a specific dish, e.g., 'Sushi'
2. Introduce a GPS signal loss (e.g., move to an area with no coverage)
3. Observe if the app displays a warning message or alternative location options
4. Re-establish GPS signal and observe if the search results are loaded successfully
5. Verify that the search history is preserved across GPS signal losses
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a warning message and alternative location options upon GPS signal loss, and recover successfully when the signal is re-established. Search history should be preserved.

test_data.expected_gps_loss_type: Move to an area with no coverage

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Free-text search with network type variations
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate free-text search functionality across different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G).
preconditions: Install the mobile app on an iPhone and a Google Pixel
Ensure GPS is enabled and location services are allowed by the app
test_steps: Step 1: Connect the iPhone to a WiFi network and open the restaurant food service app
Step 2: Tap on the search icon located at the top of the home screen and enter a specific keyword (e.g., 'Mexican') in the search bar
Step 3: Tap 'Search' and verify that the search results display restaurants related to the entered keyword within 5 seconds
Step 4: Disconnect the iPhone from WiFi and connect it to a 4G network
Step 5: Repeat steps 2-3 on the Google Pixel connected to a 4G network and verify that the search results display restaurants related to the entered keyword within 10 seconds
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results display at least 10 relevant restaurants within 5 seconds on WiFi and within 10 seconds on 4G network.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Free-text search for restaurant names
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the free-text search endpoint returns correct results based on user input.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/restaurant with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'query': 'Italian'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurant objects
5. Verify each restaurant object includes fields: id, name, cuisine, rating
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response should contain exactly 3 Italian restaurants with all required fields.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search for a non-existent restaurant
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the free-text search endpoint returns an empty result when no matching restaurants are found.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/restaurant with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'query': 'Space Invaders'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body is an empty array
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response should be an empty array indicating no matching restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Search with pagination and sorting
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the free-text search endpoint supports pagination and sorting of results.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/search/restaurant?query=Italian&page=1&pageSize=2&sortField=name&sortOrder=asc with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
3. Verify response body contains an array of 2 restaurant objects
4. Verify each restaurant object includes fields: id, name, cuisine, rating
5. Verify the first restaurant's name is alphabetically less than or equal to the second
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Response should contain exactly 2 Italian restaurants sorted by name in ascending order.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Free-text search for restaurants within a specified distance
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates that the free-text search returns only restaurants within the specified distance and sorts them correctly.
preconditions: User is logged in
Database contains at least 5 restaurants with known distances from the user's location
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile app and navigate to the 'Search' page
2. Enter a free-text search query for 'Italian food'
3. Select the option to filter by distance within 5 kilometers
4. Verify that only restaurants within 5 kilometers are displayed in the search results
5. Sort the search results by distance from nearest to farthest
6. Confirm that the restaurants are listed in ascending order of their distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Search results contain only 3 Italian restaurants within 5 kilometers, sorted by distance from nearest to farthest.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in restaurant details form
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensures that all required fields are validated when submitting a new restaurant entry.
preconditions: User is logged in with admin privileges
Database contains at least one existing restaurant
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Add Restaurant' page
2. Fill out the form with valid data for all mandatory fields (e.g., name, address, cuisine type)
3. Leave one of the mandatory fields empty and attempt to submit the form
4. Verify that an error message is displayed for the empty field
5. Correct the empty field and submit the form again
6. Confirm that the new restaurant entry is successfully created in the database
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for empty mandatory field, and successful creation of new restaurant entry upon correction.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of data type constraints for user ratings
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensures that user ratings are validated as integers within a specified range (1 to 5).
preconditions: User is logged in
Database contains at least one existing restaurant with a rating
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'View Restaurant' page for an existing restaurant
2. Attempt to update the user rating to a non-integer value (e.g., 3.5)
3. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
4. Correct the rating to an integer within the valid range (1 to 5)
5. Submit the updated rating and verify that it is successfully saved in the database
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for non-integer rating, and successful update of rating to a valid integer value.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Free-text Search

App is open and on the search page
test_steps: 1. Perform a free-text search for 'Italian restaurants'

4. Wait for 5 seconds and observe if the app automatically reconnects to the network
5. Verify that the search results are displayed again without any loss of data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display an error message, then automatically reconnect and show the search results within 5 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Free-text Search

5. Wait for 10 seconds and observe if the search results are displayed again without any loss of data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display an error message, then automatically recover and show the search results within 10 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data Persistence Across App Crashes During Free-text Search

Step 4: Tap on the cart icon located at the bottom of the screen to view the cart contents
Step 5: Proceed to checkout and enter invalid data, e.g., an empty address field
