phase2_test_cases.test_title: iOS vs Android - GPS Location Accuracy
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application accurately retrieves and displays user's current location on both iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: User must have location services enabled
Application must be installed and logged in
test_steps: 1. On an iOS device, open the application and navigate to the 'Location' feature
2. Allow the application to access your location
3. Record the latitude and longitude displayed on the screen
4. On an Android device, repeat steps 1-3
5. Compare the recorded coordinates from both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The GPS location accuracy should be within ±10 meters on both iOS and Android platforms.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Different Screen Sizes - UI Layout
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the mobile application's UI layout is responsive and displays correctly on various screen sizes.
preconditions: Application must be installed and logged in
test_steps: 1. On an iPhone 8 (small screen), open the application
2. Navigate to the 'Menu' page
3. Verify that all menu items are visible and properly spaced
4. On a Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+ (large screen), repeat steps 1-3
5. Compare the UI layout on both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application's UI should be responsive and display correctly on both iPhone 8 (small screen) and Samsung Galaxy Tab S7+ (large screen).

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Type Variations - Performance
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the mobile application performs well under different network conditions (WiFi, 4G, 5G).
preconditions: Application must be installed and logged in
Device must have internet access
test_steps: 1. On a device connected to WiFi, open the application and navigate to the 'Order' page
2. Place an order for a meal
3. Record the time taken to complete the order process
4. Switch to 4G network and repeat steps 1-3
5. Compare the time taken in both network conditions
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should perform within 20 seconds for order processing under both WiFi and 4G network conditions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create a new restaurant order
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a new restaurant order can be created successfully and includes all necessary details.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Restaurant ID is known
test_steps: 1. Send POST request to /api/orders with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include request body: {"restaurant_id": "12345", "items": [{"item_id": "67890", "quantity": 2}], "total": 25.00}
3. Verify HTTP 201 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains created order with auto-generated ID
5. Verify response body includes 'created_at' timestamp
6. Verify order appears in GET /api/orders?restaurant_id=12345 response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order is created successfully with all necessary details and appears in the order list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Update an existing restaurant order status
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that an existing restaurant order's status can be updated successfully.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Order ID is known
test_steps: 1. Send PUT request to /api/orders/<order_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include request body: {"status": "completed"}
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains updated order details
5. Verify 'updated_at' timestamp is present in the response
6. Verify order status appears as 'completed' in GET /api/orders/<order_id> response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order status is updated to 'completed' and the change is reflected in the order details.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Delete a restaurant order
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that an existing restaurant order can be deleted successfully.
preconditions: User is authenticated with valid JWT token
Order ID is known
test_steps: 1. Send DELETE request to /api/orders/<order_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 204 status code returned
3. Attempt to retrieve the order using GET /api/orders/<order_id>
4. Verify HTTP 404 status code is returned
5. Verify no order details are found in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Order is deleted successfully and attempting to retrieve it returns a 404 error.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields during user registration
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all required fields are validated and prevent submission if any field is missing.
preconditions: User is on the registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Register' page
Step 2: Attempt to submit the form without entering a username
Step 3: Verify that an error message appears indicating that the username field is required
Step 4: Enter a valid username and attempt to submit the form again
Step 5: Verify that the form submits successfully with no errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The registration form should display an error message for missing username and prevent submission. Upon entering a valid username, the form should submit successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of email format during user registration
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the email field accepts only valid email addresses and rejects invalid ones.
preconditions: User is on the registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Register' page
Step 2: Enter an invalid email format (e.g., 'test@example') in the email field
Step 3: Attempt to submit the form
Step 4: Verify that an error message appears indicating that the email format is incorrect
Step 5: Enter a valid email address and attempt to submit the form again
Step 6: Verify that the form submits successfully with no errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The registration form should display an error message for invalid email format and prevent submission. Upon entering a valid email address, the form should submit successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of password strength during user registration
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the password field requires a minimum length and contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one number.
preconditions: User is on the registration page
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Register' page
Step 2: Enter a password that does not meet the strength requirements (e.g., 'password') in the password field
Step 3: Attempt to submit the form
Step 4: Verify that an error message appears indicating that the password is too weak
Step 5: Enter a valid password meeting all strength requirements and attempt to submit the form again
Step 6: Verify that the form submits successfully with no errors
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The registration form should display an error message for weak password and prevent submission. Upon entering a valid password meeting all strength requirements, the form should submit successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Data Submission
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from network interruptions during data submission and resubmit the data successfully.
preconditions: User is logged in
App has a pending order
test_steps: Step 1: User initiates an order with specific items (e.g., 3 burgers, 2 fries)
Step 2: Network connection is interrupted while the order is being submitted
Step 3: Wait for the network to reconnect
Step 4: Verify that the app detects the reconnection and resubmits the pending order
Step 5: Confirm that the order status changes from 'Pending' to 'Confirmed' on the server
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should detect network reconnection and resubmit the pending order successfully with the correct status.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Data Persistence
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from crashes and retains all data across sessions.
preconditions: User has logged in with specific credentials
test_steps: Step 1: User logs into the app using their credentials (e.g., username: user123, password: pass456)
Step 2: User adds an item to the cart (e.g., a pizza)
Step 3: Simulate an app crash by force stopping the app
Step 4: Reopen the app and log in with the same credentials
Step 5: Verify that the added pizza is still present in the cart
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from crashes and retain all data, including items added to the cart.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Data Entry
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app handles session timeouts gracefully and allows data entry after re-authentication.
preconditions: User is logged in with specific credentials
test_steps: Step 1: User logs into the app using their credentials (e.g., username: user789, password: pass012)
Step 2: Start entering data for a new order (e.g., selecting items and quantities)
Step 3: Wait for the session to timeout (e.g., after 5 minutes of inactivity)
Step 4: Re-enter the same data and complete the order
Step 5: Verify that the order is successfully submitted with all entered data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should handle session timeouts gracefully and allow data entry after re-authentication to complete the order successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting restaurant results by rating in descending order

At least 10 restaurants are available with ratings

Step 3: Select 'Descending' from the sorting options
Step 4: Verify that the first restaurant listed has the highest rating
Step 5: Check that each subsequent restaurant has a lower rating than the previous one
Step 6: Confirm that the sorting is consistent across multiple page loads
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The restaurants are sorted by rating in descending order within 2 seconds, with the highest rated restaurant listed first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurants by cuisine type and sorting by distance

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting user reviews by helpfulness and filtering by rating

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurant results by rating in descending order
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant results by rating in descending order when requested.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?sort=rating&order=desc
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify the first restaurant in the array has the highest rating among all restaurants
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The first restaurant in the response should have the highest rating, and the list should be sorted by rating in descending order.

3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify all restaurants in the response have 'Italian' as their cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The response should contain only restaurants with 'Italian' as their cuisine type.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort and filter restaurants by rating and cuisine type

2. Include query parameters: ?sort=rating&order=desc&cuisine=Italian
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify the first restaurant in the array has the highest rating among Italian restaurants
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The response should contain only Italian restaurants, sorted by rating in descending order, with the first restaurant having the highest rating.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection Flaws in Sorting Parameter
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the sorting parameter is properly sanitized to prevent SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: Application is running with default settings
Database contains test data
test_steps: 1. Log in as a regular user and navigate to the search results page.
2. Modify the URL query parameter for sorting, e.g., append '?sort=price%27--' to the URL.
3. Observe if the application returns an error or displays unexpected data.
4. Verify that the database is not accessed with the injected SQL command.
5. Confirm that the search results are displayed without any anomalies.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return an error or display a generic message, and the database should not be accessed with the injected SQL command.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the sorting results are properly encoded to prevent XSS attacks.
preconditions: Application is running with default settings
User has access to search results
test_steps: 1. Log in as a regular user and navigate to the search results page.
2. Modify the URL query parameter for sorting, e.g., append '?sort=<script>alert('XSS')</script>' to the URL.
3. Observe if the browser displays an alert box with 'XSS'.
4. Verify that the sorting parameter is properly encoded in the HTML output.
5. Confirm that no malicious scripts are executed on the client side.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The browser should display an alert box with 'XSS', and no malicious scripts should be executed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Denial of Service (DoS) via Large Sorting Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application can handle large sorting requests without crashing or slowing down.
preconditions: Application is running with default settings
User has access to search results
test_steps: 1. Log in as a regular user and navigate to the search results page.
2. Modify the URL query parameter for sorting, e.g., append '?sort=price&limit=10000' to the URL.
3. Observe if the application responds within an acceptable time frame (e.g., less than 5 seconds).
4. Verify that the search results are displayed without any errors or crashes.
5. Confirm that the application remains responsive and does not consume excessive resources.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should respond within an acceptable time frame, display search results without errors, and remain responsive.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting results on different screen sizes

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting results with network type variations

Ensure the same device is also connected to 4G and 5G networks
test_steps: 1. On the device, open the app and navigate to the results page while connected to WiFi

3. Observe that the results are sorted based on distance from the user's location
4. Disconnect from WiFi and reconnect to 4G network, repeat steps 1-3
5. Verify that the results are still sorted based on distance from the user's location
6. Disconnect from 4G and reconnect to 5G network, repeat steps 1-3
7. Verify that the results are still sorted based on distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display results sorted by distance from the user's location regardless of the network type (WiFi, 4G, 5G).

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurant results by distance in ascending order
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant results by distance from the user's location in ascending order.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid JWT token
User has provided their current location
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'sort_by': 'distance'
3. Include query parameter 'order': 'asc'
4. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
5. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants sorted by distance in ascending order
6. Verify the first restaurant is closest to the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of restaurants sorted by distance in ascending order with the closest restaurant first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter restaurant results by cuisine type

User has provided their current location
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'cuisine': 'Italian'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants that serve Italian cuisine
5. Verify no restaurants from other cuisines are included in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of restaurants serving Italian cuisine only.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort and filter restaurant results by rating

User has provided their current location

2. Include query parameter 'sort_by': 'rating'
3. Include query parameter 'order': 'desc'
4. Include query parameter 'min_rating': 4
5. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
6. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants sorted by rating in descending order with a minimum rating of 4
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of highly-rated restaurants (minimum rating of 4) sorted by rating in descending order.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting results by price in ascending order

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Sorting Results

App is currently displaying sorted restaurant results

4. Re-establish network connection and observe if the sorting remains intact
5. Verify that all restaurants are still displayed in the sorted order

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Sorting Results

App is currently displaying sorted restaurant results

3. Reopen the app and observe if the sorting remains intact
4. Verify that all restaurants are still displayed in the sorted order
5. Check if any data is lost or corrupted
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from crashes and maintain the sorted restaurant results without losing any data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling During Sorting Results

App is currently displaying sorted restaurant results

3. Attempt to interact with the app (e.g., tap on a restaurant)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Injection Flaws in Sorting Input
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application does not allow SQL injection or other injection attacks through sorting parameters.
preconditions: Application is running with default settings
User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query into the sorting input field (e.g., '1 ORDER BY id --')
Step 3: Submit the form with the malicious input
Step 4: Observe if the application returns an error message or unexpected behavior
Step 5: Verify that no sensitive data is exposed in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should return an error message and not expose any sensitive data, indicating successful prevention of SQL injection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Sorting Results

User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search results page
Step 2: Enter a malicious script into the sorting input field (e.g., '<script>alert("XSS")</script>')
Step 3: Submit the form with the malicious input
Step 4: Observe if the application displays an alert box or unexpected behavior
Step 5: Verify that no sensitive data is exposed in the response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should display a warning message and not execute any malicious script, indicating successful prevention of XSS.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for Denial of Service (DoS) Through Sorting Requests

Step 3: Monitor the application's response time and resource usage
Step 4: Observe if the application crashes or becomes unresponsive
Step 5: Verify that the application remains responsive and performs within expected limits
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should handle 500 sorting requests without crashing, with a response time of less than 2 seconds per request.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurant results by distance ascending
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant results by distance in ascending order.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?sort=distance&order=asc
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurant objects
5. Verify the first restaurant in the array has the shortest distance from the user's location
6. Verify the last restaurant in the array has the longest distance from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of restaurants sorted by distance in ascending order with the closest restaurant first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter restaurants by cuisine type

3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurant objects
5. Verify all restaurants in the array have cuisine type 'Italian'
6. Verify no restaurants with other cuisine types are included
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns a list of Italian restaurants only.

3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurant objects

3. Select 'Distance Near to Far'
4. Verify that the results are sorted correctly by distance in ascending order
5. On the Android device, repeat steps 1-4 and ensure the sorting is consistent
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Results are sorted correctly by distance in ascending order on both devices across different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G).

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort restaurant results by distance ascending
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant results based on distance in ascending order.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
There are multiple restaurants within range
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?sort=distance&order=asc
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify the first restaurant in the array is closest to the user's location
6. Verify the last restaurant in the array is farthest from the user's location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns restaurants sorted by distance in ascending order, with the closest restaurant first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filter restaurants by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API filters restaurant results based on the specified cuisine type.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
There are multiple restaurants of different cuisines
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter: ?cuisine=Italian
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify all restaurants in the array are Italian cuisine
6. Verify the number of Italian restaurants matches expected count
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns only Italian cuisine restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sort and filter combined: Distance ascending, Cuisine type Italian
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API sorts restaurant results by distance in ascending order and filters by cuisine type.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
There are multiple restaurants of different cuisines within range
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameters: ?sort=distance&order=asc&cuisine=Italian
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains an array of restaurants
5. Verify the first restaurant in the array is closest to the user's location and is Italian cuisine
6. Verify the last restaurant in the array is farthest from the user's location and is Italian cuisine
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns Italian cuisine restaurants sorted by distance in ascending order, with the closest restaurant first.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting results by price in ascending order
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates that the mobile application sorts restaurant items correctly by price in ascending order.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the 'Menu' page
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Menu' tab
Step 2: Scroll down and select a category of food items
Step 3: Tap on the sorting icon (usually three dots or an arrow) located at the top right corner of the menu list
Step 4: Select 'Price - Low to High' from the dropdown menu
Step 5: Verify that the list of items is sorted by price in ascending order
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The list of restaurant items displays correctly, with prices increasing from top to bottom.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields during order placement
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensures that all required fields are validated when placing an order in the mobile application.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the 'Menu' page
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Menu' tab
Step 2: Select a food item and add it to the cart
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Cart' page
Step 4: Attempt to proceed to checkout without filling out all required fields (e.g., name, address)
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed for each missing mandatory field
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays specific error messages indicating which fields are required and must be filled out.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of data type constraints for user input
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensures that the mobile application correctly validates data types for user inputs such as phone number and email.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
The user navigates to the 'Profile' page
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Profile' tab
Step 2: Edit the 'Phone Number' field and enter a non-numeric value (e.g., letters)
Step 3: Attempt to save changes
Step 4: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that phone number must be numeric
Step 5: Edit the 'Email' field and enter an invalid email format (e.g., missing '@' symbol)
Step 6: Attempt to save changes
Step 7: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that email format is incorrect
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application displays specific error messages indicating that phone number must be numeric and email format is incorrect.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during sorting results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies the application handles network interruptions gracefully while sorting results.
preconditions: User is logged in
Application is open and displaying search results
test_steps: 1. Sort results by 'Rating' in ascending order.
2. Introduce a network interruption (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi or switch to airplane mode).
3. Observe if the sorting operation continues without freezing or crashing.
4. Wait for 10 seconds after the interruption.
5. Verify that the results are still sorted by 'Rating' and no new data is fetched.
6. Reconnect to the network and verify that the application does not crash.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorting operation completes successfully without freezing or crashing, and results remain sorted by 'Rating' after reconnecting to the network.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after sorting results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the application recovers from crashes during sorting operations.
preconditions: User is logged in
Application is open and displaying search results
test_steps: 1. Sort results by 'Price' in descending order.
2. Introduce a crash (e.g., force stop the app or simulate a crash).
3. Observe if the application crashes during sorting.
4. Wait for 5 seconds after the crash.
5. Reopen the application and verify that it does not crash upon reopening.
6. Check if the results are still sorted by 'Price' and no new data is fetched.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application recovers from crashes during sorting operations, and results remain sorted by 'Price' after reopening.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS signal loss behavior during sorting results
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies the application handles GPS signal loss gracefully while sorting results.
preconditions: User is logged in
Application is open and displaying search results
test_steps: 1. Sort results by 'Distance' from user's location.
2. Introduce a GPS signal loss (e.g., switch to airplane mode or block GPS access).
3. Observe if the sorting operation continues without freezing or crashing.
4. Wait for 10 seconds after the signal loss.
5. Verify that the results are still sorted by 'Distance' and no new data is fetched.
6. Reconnect to the GPS signal and verify that the application does not crash.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The sorting operation completes successfully without freezing or crashing, and results remain sorted by 'Distance' after reconnecting to the GPS signal.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - High concurrency
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under high user load when downloading and notifying users of new releases.
preconditions: System is up and running
Initial set of users with valid accounts
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 concurrent users accessing the download page
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that all users receive notifications within 5 seconds of a new release being available
Step 4: Check that no more than 10% of requests result in errors or timeouts
Step 5: Confirm that the system remains stable and responsive throughout the test duration
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System handles 500 concurrent users with a notification response time of less than 5 seconds and an error rate below 10%

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Large file download
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance when downloading large files to users and ensuring timely notifications.
preconditions: System is up and running
Initial set of users with valid accounts
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate a user initiating the download of a 500MB file
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that the download completes within 10 minutes
Step 4: Check that the notification is sent to the user within 2 minutes of the download completing
Step 5: Confirm that the system remains stable and responsive throughout the test duration
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System handles a 500MB file download within 10 minutes and sends a notification within 2 minutes

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Stress test with large user base
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under stress when handling a large number of concurrent users downloading and being notified of new releases.
preconditions: System is up and running
Initial set of 10,000 users with valid accounts
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 5,000 concurrent users accessing the download page
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that all users receive notifications within 7 seconds of a new release being available
Step 4: Check that no more than 5% of requests result in errors or timeouts
Step 5: Confirm that the system remains stable and responsive throughout the test duration
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System handles 5,000 concurrent users with a notification response time of less than 7 seconds and an error rate below 5%

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that users are notified of new releases via email
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users receive an email notification when a new release is available.
preconditions: User must have an active account with the restaurant service
test_steps: 1. Log in to the user's account using their credentials.
2. Navigate to the 'Settings' or 'Profile' page.
3. Enable the 'Email Notifications' option if it is not already enabled.
4. Wait for a new release to be uploaded by the restaurant service.
5. Check the user's email inbox for a notification about the new release.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user receives an email with the subject 'New Release Available' containing details of the new release.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure that users cannot bypass email notification settings
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users cannot bypass or disable their email notifications for new releases.
preconditions: User must have an active account with the restaurant service
test_steps: 1. Log in to the user's account using their credentials.
2. Navigate to the 'Settings' or 'Profile' page.
3. Attempt to disable the 'Email Notifications' option.
4. Confirm that the system prevents the user from disabling email notifications.
5. Verify that the 'Email Notifications' option remains enabled.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents the user from disabling their email notifications for new releases, and the option remains enabled.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that users receive notifications only once per release
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users receive a notification for each new release only once.
preconditions: User must have an active account with the restaurant service
test_steps: 1. Log in to the user's account using their credentials.
2. Navigate to the 'Settings' or 'Profile' page and enable email notifications.
3. Wait for two new releases to be uploaded by the restaurant service.
4. Check the user's email inbox for notifications about both releases.
5. Verify that each release is notified only once.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user receives notifications for each new release only once, with distinct subjects for each release.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clear and intuitive navigation to the download page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure users can easily navigate to the download page from the main menu.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner
test_steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Settings' icon located in the top right corner of the dashboard.
Step 2: Scroll down and click on the 'Software Updates' option.
Step 3: Verify that the 'Download New Release' button is prominently displayed on the page.
Step 4: Click on the 'Download New Release' button and verify that it navigates to a new tab or modal with the download options.
Step 5: Confirm that the navigation process takes no more than 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User should be able to navigate to the download page within 2 seconds and see a prominent 'Download New Release' button.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify clear error message when no new releases are available
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure users receive a clear and informative error message if there are no new software updates available.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner
test_steps: Step 1: Click on the 'Settings' icon located in the top right corner of the dashboard.
Step 2: Scroll down and click on the 'Software Updates' option.
Step 3: Simulate no new releases by setting up a test environment where there are no updates available.
Step 4: Click on the 'Download New Release' button and observe the error message displayed.
Step 5: Verify that the error message is clear, concise, and instructive (e.g., 'No new software updates available at this time.')
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User should receive a clear and informative error message indicating that there are no new software updates available.

test_data.expected_error_message: No new software updates available at this time.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify mobile vs desktop experience differences for download page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the download page is optimized for both mobile and desktop devices.
preconditions: User is logged in as a restaurant owner
test_steps: Step 1: Access the 'Settings' icon on a mobile device and click on it.
Step 2: Scroll down and click on the 'Software Updates' option.
Step 3: Verify that the download page is fully functional and easy to navigate on the mobile device.
Step 4: Access the 'Settings' icon on a desktop device and click on it.
Step 5: Scroll down and click on the 'Software Updates' option.
Step 6: Verify that the download page is fully functional and easy to navigate on the desktop device.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The download page should be fully functional and easy to navigate on both mobile and desktop devices.

test_data.expected_mobile_experience: Fully functional and easy to navigate
test_data.expected_desktop_experience: Fully functional and easy to navigate

phase2_test_cases.test_title: iOS vs Android - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the app correctly downloads and notifies users about new releases on both iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
App is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. On an iPhone, open the app and navigate to the 'Settings' page.
2. Tap on 'Check for Updates'.
3. Wait for the update process to complete.
4. Once updated, go back to the main screen of the app.
5. Verify that a notification appears indicating the new version is available and prompt to download.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Notification with 'New Version Available' and 'Download Now' button appears on both iOS and Android devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Different Screen Sizes/Resolutions - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the app correctly displays download notifications on various screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
App is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. On a device with a large screen (e.g., iPad), open the app and navigate to the 'Settings' page.
2. Tap on 'Check for Updates'.
3. Wait for the update process to complete.
4. Once updated, go back to the main screen of the app.
5. Verify that a notification appears indicating the new version is available and prompt to download.
6. On a device with a smaller screen (e.g., iPhone SE), repeat steps 1-5.
7. Verify that the same notification appears on both devices.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Notification with 'New Version Available' and 'Download Now' button appears consistently across different screen sizes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Browser Compatibility - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the web version of the app correctly downloads and notifies users about new releases across different browsers.
preconditions: User has an active internet connection
Web browser is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. Open a web browser (e.g., Chrome) on a desktop computer.
2. Navigate to the app's website and log in.
3. Go to the 'Settings' page.
4. Click on 'Check for Updates'.
5. Wait for the update process to complete.
6. Once updated, go back to the main screen of the web app.
7. Verify that a notification appears indicating the new version is available and prompt to download.
8. Repeat steps 1-7 using a different browser (e.g., Firefox).
9. Verify that the same notification appears on both browsers.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Notification with 'New Version Available' and 'Download Now' button appears consistently across different web browsers.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify successful download of new release notification
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API correctly downloads and notifies users of new releases.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
New release data is available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/releases with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'lastChecked': '2023-04-01T00:00:00Z'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains new release data with expected fields (title, version, releaseDate)
5. Verify the new release appears in the user's notification list on the UI
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns new release data and updates user notification list successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify handling of no new releases found
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API correctly handles scenarios where there are no new releases.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
No new release data available
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/releases with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'lastChecked': '2023-04-01T00:00:00Z'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body is an empty array
5. Verify the user's notification list remains unchanged on the UI
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API returns an empty array and does not update user notification list.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify rate limiting for release downloads
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the API correctly implements rate limiting for release download requests.
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid Bearer token
New release data is available
test_steps: 1. Send 5 GET requests to /api/releases with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include query parameter 'lastChecked': '2023-04-01T00:00:00Z'
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code for the first request
4. Verify HTTP 429 status code for subsequent requests
5. Wait for rate limit reset and verify HTTP 200 status code on next attempt
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: API correctly implements rate limiting and returns appropriate HTTP status codes.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify new release download notification for users
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that when a new release is available, users are notified via email and the system logs the event.
preconditions: A new release version is uploaded to the server
test_steps: 1. Log in as an admin user with appropriate permissions
2. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the admin dashboard
3. Click on the 'Upload New Release' button and upload a new version of the software
4. Confirm that the system logs the new release upload event in the system log file
5. Check the email inbox of all users to verify that they receive an email notification about the new release
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system logs the new release upload event and all users receive an email notification with details of the new version.

test_data.expected_log_message: New release v2.0.1 uploaded by admin on [current date and time]

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate mandatory fields for new release upload
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all mandatory fields are validated when a user attempts to upload a new release.
preconditions: A user is logged in with appropriate permissions
test_steps: 1. Log in as a user with appropriate permissions
2. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the admin dashboard
3. Click on the 'Upload New Release' button and leave all mandatory fields empty
4. Attempt to upload the release by clicking the 'Submit' button
5. Verify that an error message is displayed for each mandatory field indicating that it cannot be left blank
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message for each mandatory field when they are left empty during the new release upload process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure accurate calculation of download count for a release
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system accurately calculates and updates the download count for each release.
preconditions: A user has downloaded a release
test_steps: 1. Log in as an admin user with appropriate permissions
2. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the admin dashboard
3. Locate the release that was recently downloaded by a user and note its current download count
4. Simulate another user downloading the same release
5. Verify that the system updates the download count for the release by 1
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system accurately calculates and updates the download count for each release after a new download occurs.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the app handles network interruptions gracefully during release download and notifies users.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release is available
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release on a stable internet connection.
2. Introduce a simulated network interruption (e.g., using a tool like Network Link Conditioner).
3. Observe the app's behavior during the interruption.
4. Wait for the network to resume and observe if the download continues seamlessly.
5. Verify that after resuming, the user is notified of the successful completion of the download.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should pause the download, handle the interruption gracefully, and resume upon network recovery. The user should receive a notification indicating successful completion.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the app recovers from crashes during release download and resumes seamlessly.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release is available
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release on a stable internet connection.
2. Simulate an app crash (e.g., by force quitting the app from the device).
3. Restart the app and observe if it resumes the download from where it left off.
4. Wait for the download to complete and verify that the user is notified of successful completion.
5. Check if the downloaded file is intact and functional.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from crashes, resume the download seamlessly, and notify the user of successful completion. The downloaded file should be intact and functional.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior During Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the app handles GPS signal loss during release download and notifies users.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release is available
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release while connected to a stable internet connection.
2. Simulate GPS signal loss (e.g., by turning off the device's GPS or moving into an area with poor signal).
3. Observe the app's behavior during the signal loss.
4. Wait for the GPS signal to return and observe if the download continues seamlessly.
5. Verify that after resuming, the user is notified of the successful completion of the download.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should pause the download during GPS signal loss, handle the interruption gracefully, and resume upon signal recovery. The user should receive a notification indicating successful completion.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - High concurrency scenario
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under high user load for downloading and notifying users of new releases.
preconditions: System is up and running
Initial set of users have been registered
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 500 concurrent users accessing the download page
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that all users receive notifications for new releases within 5 seconds
Step 4: Check that no errors or timeouts occur in the system logs
Step 5: Confirm that the download functionality is not impacted and completes successfully
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System maintains stable performance with no errors, all users receive notifications within 5 seconds

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Large file download scenario
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance when downloading large files to multiple users simultaneously.
