Initial set of users have been registered
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 200 concurrent users downloading a 50MB file
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that all downloads complete successfully within 60 seconds
Step 4: Check that no errors or timeouts occur in the system logs
Step 5: Confirm that notifications for new releases are sent after file download completes
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System maintains stable performance with no errors, all downloads complete within 60 seconds and notifications are sent correctly

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download and notify users of new releases - Stress test with large number of users
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under extreme load when a large number of users attempt to download and receive notifications simultaneously.
preconditions: System is up and running
Initial set of users have been registered
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate 10,000 concurrent users accessing the download page
Step 2: Monitor system performance metrics (CPU, memory, network) during the test
Step 3: Verify that all users receive notifications for new releases within 10 seconds
Step 4: Check that no errors or timeouts occur in the system logs
Step 5: Confirm that download functionality is not impacted and completes successfully
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System maintains stable performance with no errors, all users receive notifications within 10 seconds

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that users cannot download new releases without proper authentication
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that only authenticated users can access and download new release notifications.
preconditions: User is not logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download New Releases' page.
Step 2: Attempt to click on the 'Download' button.
Step 3: Verify that a login prompt appears.
Step 4: Enter valid credentials and attempt to log in.
Step 5: After successful login, verify that the download link is accessible.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User must be prompted to log in before accessing the download link.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure that users cannot bypass release notification restrictions
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users cannot access new releases without being notified.
preconditions: User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download New Releases' page.
Step 2: Verify that no new releases are displayed.
Step 3: Wait for a new release to be available.
Step 4: Refresh the 'Download New Releases' page after the new release is available.
Step 5: Verify that the new release notification appears and can be downloaded.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Users should only see new releases after being notified.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that users cannot download new releases from unauthorized devices
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users can only access and download new release notifications from authorized devices.
preconditions: User is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the 'Download New Releases' page on a device with valid authorization.
Step 2: Attempt to navigate to the same page on an unauthorized device.
Step 3: Verify that access is denied and a login prompt appears.
Step 4: Enter valid credentials and attempt to log in on the unauthorized device.
Step 5: After successful login, verify that the download link is accessible.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Users must be prompted to log in before accessing the download link on unauthorized devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify that users are notified of new releases when downloading an app
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the user interface clearly displays a notification for new app versions and provides a clear path to download the update.
preconditions: User has the current version of the app installed
A new version of the app is available
test_steps: 1. Open the app on a mobile device
2. Navigate to the settings or main menu
3. Tap on 'Check for Updates'
4. Verify that a notification appears indicating a new version is available
5. Confirm that the notification includes details such as the version number and size of the update
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A clear notification is displayed with the new version number and update size, prompting the user to download the update.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Ensure that users can easily navigate to the download page from the notification
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the notification provides a direct link or button for users to access the app store and download the latest version.
preconditions: User has received a notification about a new app update
test_steps: 1. Open the app on a mobile device
2. Navigate to the settings or main menu
3. Tap on 'Check for Updates'
4. Verify that a notification appears indicating a new version is available
5. Tap on the 'Download Now' button within the notification
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Tapping the 'Download Now' button takes the user directly to the app store page where they can download the latest version of the app.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validate that error messages are clear and actionable when a user tries to download an update without internet connectivity
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the app provides a clear and actionable error message when a user attempts to download an update but is not connected to the internet.
preconditions: User has received a notification about a new app update
Device is offline
test_steps: 1. Open the app on a mobile device
2. Navigate to the settings or main menu
3. Tap on 'Check for Updates'
4. Verify that a notification appears indicating a new version is available
5. Tap on the 'Download Now' button within the notification
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app displays a clear error message indicating that there is no internet connection, along with an actionable option such as 'Retry' or 'Check Connection'.

test_data.offline_error_message: No internet connection. Please connect to the internet and try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: iOS vs Android - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the app correctly downloads and notifies users about new releases on both iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: User has an active account
App is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. On an iPhone, open the app and navigate to the 'Settings' page.
2. Tap on 'Check for Updates'.
3. Wait for the update process to complete.
4. Verify that a notification appears indicating the availability of a new release.
5. Tap on the notification and confirm that the download begins automatically.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A notification appears indicating a new release, and the app downloads it automatically.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Different Screen Sizes/Resolutions - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the app correctly downloads and notifies users about new releases on devices with different screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: User has an active account
App is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. On a device with a large screen (e.g., iPad), open the app and navigate to the 'Settings' page.
2. Tap on 'Check for Updates'.
3. Wait for the update process to complete.
4. Verify that a notification appears indicating the availability of a new release.
5. Tap on the notification and confirm that the download begins automatically.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A notification appears indicating a new release, and the app downloads it automatically on both large and small screen devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Browser Compatibility - Download and Notify Users of New Releases
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the web-based version of the app correctly downloads and notifies users about new releases on different browsers.
preconditions: User has an active account
Web browser is installed and updated to the latest version
test_steps: 1. Open a web browser (e.g., Chrome, Firefox) and navigate to the app's website.
2. Log in with the user's credentials.
3. Navigate to the 'Settings' page.
4. Tap on 'Check for Updates'.
5. Wait for the update process to complete.
6. Verify that a notification appears indicating the availability of a new release.
7. Tap on the notification and confirm that the download begins automatically.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A notification appears indicating a new release, and the app downloads it automatically on both Chrome and Firefox browsers.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify successful download of new release notification
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that users receive a notification when a new release is available.
preconditions: User must be logged in with valid credentials
A new release must be uploaded to the system
test_steps: 1. Log in as a user using their valid username and password
2. Navigate to the 'Notifications' section of the application
3. Send a GET request to /api/releases/latest with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
4. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
5. Verify response body contains the latest release details including version number and download link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User receives a notification with the latest release details (version 1.2.3, download link provided)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify rate limiting for new release notifications
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the system limits the number of new release notifications a user can receive in a given time frame.
preconditions: User must be logged in with valid credentials
Multiple new releases must be uploaded to the system
test_steps: 1. Log in as a user using their valid username and password
2. Navigate to the 'Notifications' section of the application
3. Send 5 GET requests to /api/releases/latest with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token> within a 1-minute interval
4. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned for each request
5. Verify response body contains the latest release details including version number and download link
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User receives up to 5 new release notifications within a 1-minute interval (rate limit of 5 requests)

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Verify authentication for new release notifications
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that only authenticated users can receive new release notifications.
preconditions: A new release must be uploaded to the system
test_steps: 1. Attempt to send a GET request to /api/releases/latest without any authorization header
2. Verify HTTP 401 status code is returned
3. Log in as a user using their valid username and password
4. Send a GET request to /api/releases/latest with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
5. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response, while authenticated users receive the latest release details

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields during new release download notification
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all required fields are validated when a user attempts to download and notify about new releases.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release information is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the application.
2. Click on the 'Download New Release' button for a specific release.
3. Attempt to submit the form without filling out any mandatory fields.
4. Verify that an error message is displayed for each missing mandatory field.
5. Fill in all mandatory fields with valid data and attempt to submit again.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All mandatory fields are validated, and the form can be submitted successfully with valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Calculation accuracy for release download notifications
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the calculation of download counts and notification times is accurate.
preconditions: User has downloaded a new release
Notification system is active
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the application.
2. Download a new release for a specific version.
3. Wait for the notification system to process the download event.
4. Verify that the download count for the specific release is incremented by 1.
5. Check the timestamp on the notification message and ensure it matches the current time.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The download count is incremented by 1, and the notification timestamp is accurate.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Foreign key relationships validation during new release download notifications
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the foreign key relationships between releases and users are correctly validated.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release information is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Releases' section of the application.
2. Click on the 'Download New Release' button for a specific release.
3. Verify that the user's ID is correctly associated with the download event in the database.
4. Check if the release version is correctly linked to the user who downloaded it.
5. Attempt to submit the form with a different user's ID and verify that an error message is displayed.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The foreign key relationships between users and releases are correctly validated, and the form can be submitted successfully with valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the app can handle network interruptions during release download and resume upon reconnection.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release available for download
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release on the app.
2. Introduce a network interruption (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi or mobile data).
3. Wait for the download to pause due to the interruption.
4. Reconnect to the network and observe if the download resumes automatically.
5. Verify that the download completes successfully without any data loss.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Download should pause upon network interruption and resume seamlessly upon reconnection, completing without data loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Release Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the app can recover from a crash during release download and resume upon restart.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release available for download
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release on the app.
2. Simulate an app crash (e.g., force stop the app from device settings).
3. Restart the app and observe if it resumes the download process.
4. Verify that the download completes successfully without any data loss.
5. Confirm that all downloaded files are intact and no corruption is detected.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from a crash during release download and resume seamlessly upon restart, completing without data loss.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior During Release Download
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the app handles GPS signal loss during release download and continues to function without interruption.
preconditions: User is logged in
New release available for download
test_steps: 1. Start downloading a new release on the app while connected to GPS.
2. Introduce GPS signal loss (e.g., move into an area with no coverage).
3. Observe if the app continues to function and the download process is not affected.
4. Re-establish GPS signal and verify that the download resumes automatically.
5. Confirm that the download completes successfully without any data loss.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should handle GPS signal loss during release download and continue to function seamlessly, resuming the download upon re-establishment of GPS signal.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results by cuisine type within a time limit
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application can filter restaurant results by cuisine type and return them within a specified time limit.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Find Restaurants' page.
2. Select the 'Filter by Cuisine' option.
3. Choose 'Italian' as the cuisine type from the dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Apply Filter' button.
5. Verify that the filtered results are displayed within 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filtered Italian restaurants are displayed within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results by price range and sorting by rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application can filter restaurant results by a specific price range, sort them by rating, and return them within a specified time limit.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Find Restaurants' page.
2. Select the 'Filter by Price Range' option.
3. Choose '$$' as the price range from the dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Apply Filter' button.
5. Select 'Sort by Rating' from the sorting options.
6. Verify that the filtered and sorted results are displayed within 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filtered '$$' restaurants sorted by rating are displayed within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results by distance and verifying result count
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the application can filter restaurant results by a specific distance and return the correct number of results.
preconditions: Application is running
User is logged in
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Find Restaurants' page.
2. Select the 'Filter by Distance' option.
3. Choose '5 km' as the distance from the dropdown menu.
4. Click on the 'Apply Filter' button.
5. Verify that exactly 10 restaurants are displayed within 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filtered results by distance show exactly 10 restaurants within 2 seconds.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering by Category Injection Attack
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application filters results by category safely and prevents SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged in
Category input field exists
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query into the category filter field, e.g., 'category'='X' OR '1'='1'
Step 3: Submit the filter request
Step 4: Verify that no results are returned and an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not execute any SQL commands related to the malicious input
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should reject the malicious input, display an error message, and prevent any SQL execution.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering by Price Range Injection Attack
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application filters results by price range safely and prevents SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged in
Price range filter fields exist
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query into the minimum price field, e.g., 'min_price'='-1' OR '1'='1'
Step 3: Submit the filter request
Step 4: Verify that no results are returned and an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not execute any SQL commands related to the malicious input
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should reject the malicious input, display an error message, and prevent any SQL execution.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering by Rating Injection Attack
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the application filters results by rating safely and prevents SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User is logged in
Rating filter field exists
test_steps: Step 1: Navigate to the search page
Step 2: Enter a malicious SQL query into the rating filter field, e.g., 'rating'='5' OR '1'='1'
Step 3: Submit the filter request
Step 4: Verify that no results are returned and an error message is displayed indicating invalid input
Step 5: Confirm that the application does not execute any SQL commands related to the malicious input
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The application should reject the malicious input, display an error message, and prevent any SQL execution.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering Restaurants by Cuisine Type
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users can filter restaurants by cuisine type and see the correct results.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
User has access to the restaurant list screen
test_steps: 1. Tap on the 'Filter' icon located at the top right corner of the restaurant list screen
2. Scroll down and tap on the 'Cuisine Type' filter option
3. Select 'Italian' cuisine type from the dropdown menu
4. Observe that only restaurants with Italian cuisine are displayed in the list
5. Tap on any Italian restaurant to view its details
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app displays only Italian restaurants when 'Italian' is selected as the cuisine type filter.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting Restaurants by Rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users can sort restaurants by rating and see the correct order.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
User has access to the restaurant list screen
test_steps: 1. Tap on the 'Filter' icon located at the top right corner of the restaurant list screen
2. Scroll down and tap on the 'Sort By' filter option
3. Select 'Rating (High to Low)' from the dropdown menu
4. Observe that restaurants are sorted in descending order based on their ratings
5. Tap on any restaurant to view its details
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app displays restaurants sorted in descending order based on their ratings when 'Rating (High to Low)' is selected.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Clearing Filters and Sorting Options
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that users can clear filters and sorting options to return to the default view.
preconditions: User is logged in to the mobile app
User has access to the restaurant list screen with active filters or sorting
test_steps: 1. Tap on the 'Filter' icon located at the top right corner of the restaurant list screen
2. Scroll down and tap on the 'Clear Filters' button
3. Observe that all filters are cleared and the default view is displayed
4. Tap on the 'Sort By' filter option
5. Select 'Default' from the dropdown menu
6. Observe that restaurants are displayed in their default order
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app displays restaurants in their default order when all filters and sorting options are cleared.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results on iOS device with different screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that filtering results works correctly across various iOS devices with different screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: Install the mobile app on an iPhone 12 (6.1 inches) and an iPad Pro (13-inch, M1 chip).
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on both the iPhone 12 and iPad Pro.
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page on both devices.
Step 3: Enter a search term such as 'Italian food' in the search bar.
Step 4: Tap on the filter icon (usually represented by three horizontal lines) and select 'Price Range'.
Step 5: Choose a price range, for example, '$10-$20', and tap 'Apply Filter'.
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants within the selected price range are displayed on both devices.
Step 7: Check if the layout and functionality of the filter options are consistent across both devices.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filter results display correctly on both iPhone 12 and iPad Pro with consistent layout and functionality.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results on Android device with different screen sizes
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that filtering results works correctly across various Android devices with different screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: Install the mobile app on a Samsung Galaxy S21 (6.2 inches) and a Google Pixel 5a (5.8 inches).
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on both the Samsung Galaxy S21 and Google Pixel 5a.
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page on both devices.
Step 3: Enter a search term such as 'Japanese food' in the search bar.
Step 4: Tap on the filter icon (usually represented by three horizontal lines) and select 'Cuisine Type'.
Step 5: Choose 'Japanese' cuisine type and tap 'Apply Filter'.
Step 6: Verify that only restaurants serving Japanese food are displayed on both devices.
Step 7: Check if the layout and functionality of the filter options are consistent across both devices.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filter results display correctly on both Samsung Galaxy S21 and Google Pixel 5a with consistent layout and functionality.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering results on different network types
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that filtering results works correctly across different network types (WiFi, 4G, 5G).
preconditions: Ensure the mobile device is connected to a WiFi network and then switch to a 4G/5G network.
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile application on a connected to a WiFi network.
Step 2: Navigate to the 'Search' page and enter a search term such as 'Mexican food'.
Step 3: Tap on the filter icon and select 'Rating'.
Step 4: Choose '5-star' rating and tap 'Apply Filter'.
Step 5: Verify that only restaurants with a 5-star rating are displayed.
Step 6: Switch to a 4G/5G network and repeat steps 2-5.
Step 7: Verify that the filtering results remain consistent across both network types.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Filter results display correctly on both WiFi and 4G/5G networks with consistent functionality.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurants by cuisine type
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API correctly filters restaurants based on the specified cuisine type.
preconditions: A valid Bearer token is available
The restaurant database contains at least one entry
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameter 'cuisine' set to 'Italian'
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify the response body contains only restaurants with cuisine type 'Italian'
5. Verify that the number of results does not exceed 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of Italian restaurants with HTTP status code 200 and no more than 100 results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurants by location
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API correctly filters restaurants based on the specified location.
preconditions: A valid Bearer token is available
The restaurant database contains at least one entry
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameter 'location' set to 'New York'
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify the response body contains only restaurants located in New York
5. Verify that the number of results does not exceed 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants in New York with HTTP status code 200 and no more than 100 results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering restaurants by rating
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the API correctly filters restaurants based on the specified rating.
preconditions: A valid Bearer token is available
The restaurant database contains at least one entry
test_steps: 1. Send GET request to /api/restaurants with query parameter 'rating' set to '4'
2. Include header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code is returned
4. Verify the response body contains only restaurants with a rating of at least 4
5. Verify that the number of results does not exceed 100
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a list of restaurants with a rating of at least 4 with HTTP status code 200 and no more than 100 results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering by cuisine type should return correct results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that filtering by cuisine type returns only relevant restaurant entries.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page
2. Click on the 'Filter' button
3. Select 'Cuisine Type' filter and choose 'Italian'
4. Verify that only Italian restaurants are displayed
5. Check that no other cuisine type restaurants appear in the list
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Only Italian restaurants should be visible, with a total count of at least 10.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Sorting by rating should return restaurants in descending order
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that sorting by rating returns restaurants in descending order of their ratings.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data with ratings is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page
2. Click on the 'Sort' button
3. Select 'Rating' sorting option
4. Verify that restaurants are displayed in descending order of their ratings
5. Check that the highest rated restaurant appears first
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Restaurants should be sorted by rating in descending order, with the highest rated restaurant at the top.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Filtering by price range should return correct results
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that filtering by price range returns only relevant restaurant entries within the specified range.
preconditions: User is logged in
Restaurant data with prices is available
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Restaurants' page
2. Click on the 'Filter' button
3. Select 'Price Range' filter and choose '$50-$100'
4. Verify that only restaurants within the $50-$100 price range are displayed
5. Check that no other price range restaurants appear in the list
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Only restaurants within the $50-$100 price range should be visible, with a total count of at least 8.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Data Persistence
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the app can persist data across network interruptions and recover upon reconnection.
preconditions: App is running with a list of restaurants displayed
User has selected some filters
test_steps: Step 1: Select multiple restaurants from the filter results page
Step 2: Introduce a network interruption (e.g., toggle airplane mode)
Step 3: Wait for the app to disconnect and show an offline message
Step 4: Reconnect to the network
Step 5: Verify that the selected restaurants are still displayed and filters are retained
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Selected restaurants persist across network interruptions and filters are retained upon reconnection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery After Filtering Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the app can recover from a crash after filtering results and display the correct data upon restart.
preconditions: App is running with a list of restaurants displayed
User has selected some filters
test_steps: Step 1: Select multiple restaurants from the filter results page
Step 2: Simulate an app crash (e.g., force stop via device settings)
Step 3: Restart the app and wait for it to load
Step 4: Verify that the app displays the same list of restaurants with the selected filters
Step 5: Confirm that the app does not show any error messages or incorrect data
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: App recovers from crash and displays the correct list of filtered restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior During Filtering Results
phase2_test_cases.description: Check how the app handles GPS signal loss while filtering results and whether it retains the user's location filter.
preconditions: App is running with a list of restaurants displayed
User has selected a location-based filter
test_steps: Step 1: Select a location-based filter (e.g., 'Nearby')
Step 2: Introduce GPS signal loss (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi and mobile data)
Step 3: Wait for the app to show an error message or indicate no results found
Step 4: Reconnect to a network with GPS signal
Step 5: Verify that the location-based filter is still active and the app displays restaurants within the selected location
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: App retains the location-based filter upon GPS signal loss and reconnection, displaying relevant results.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page load time under high user concurrency
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of the Profile page when accessed by a large number of users simultaneously.
preconditions: Application is up and running
User accounts are created for testing
test_steps: Step 1: Start a load test using Apache JMeter with 50 concurrent users
Step 2: Monitor the application's response time for accessing the Profile page
Step 3: Increase the number of concurrent users to 100 and observe the performance impact
Step 4: Verify that the average response time does not exceed 2 seconds at any point during the test
Step 5: Ensure that no errors or failures occur during the load test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Average response time should not exceed 2 seconds under 100 concurrent users

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page data load under heavy data usage
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of the Profile page when handling a large amount of data.
preconditions: Application is up and running
A user with a large number of profile items is created
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as the user with heavy data usage
Step 2: Navigate to the Profile page
Step 3: Verify that all profile items are displayed correctly without any errors
Step 4: Measure the time taken to load all profile items
Step 5: Ensure that the total number of profile items is within the expected limit
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All profile items should be displayed correctly and within 5 seconds

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page update performance under high load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of updating a profile item when accessed by multiple users simultaneously.
preconditions: Application is up and running
Multiple user accounts are created
test_steps: Step 1: Start a load test using Apache JMeter with 50 concurrent users
Step 2: Simulate updating a profile item for each user
Step 3: Monitor the application's response time for updating the profile item
Step 4: Increase the number of concurrent users to 100 and observe the performance impact
Step 5: Verify that the average response time does not exceed 2 seconds at any point during the test
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Average response time should not exceed 2 seconds under 100 concurrent users

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page - Injection Attack Prevention
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the profile page is protected against SQL injection attacks.
preconditions: User must be logged in with a valid account
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page using the application's navigation menu.
2. Modify the URL parameter for the user ID to include a SQL injection payload, e.g., `user_id=1' OR '1'='1`.
3. Observe the response from the server.
4. Verify that the server returns an error message or does not display sensitive data.
5. Confirm that no unauthorized access is granted to other users' profiles.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The server should return an error message and not expose any user data, preventing SQL injection.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page - Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Prevention
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the profile page is protected against XSS attacks.
preconditions: User must be logged in with a valid account
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page using the application's navigation menu.
2. Modify the user input fields (e.g., name, bio) with a script tag, e.g., `<script>alert('XSS')</script>.
3. Submit the form and observe the response from the server.
4. Verify that the script is not executed on the page.
5. Confirm that no unauthorized access or data leakage occurs.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The script should not be executed, and the application should prevent XSS attacks by sanitizing user input.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page - Password Reset Security
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the password reset functionality is secure and does not allow unauthorized access.
preconditions: User must have a valid account
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page using the application's navigation menu.
2. Click on the 'Forgot Password' link.
3. Enter an email address that is associated with a user account and submit the form.
4. Observe the response from the server.
5. Verify that a password reset email is sent to the provided email address.
6. Check the content of the email for any security tokens or links.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A secure password reset email should be sent with a valid token, and no unauthorized access to user accounts should occur.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page navigation and visibility
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the profile page is easily navigable and all necessary elements are clearly visible.
preconditions: User is logged in to the application
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Menu' icon located at the bottom of the screen.
Step 2: Scroll through the menu options until reaching the 'Profile' section.
Step 3: Tap on the 'Profile' option.
Step 4: Verify that the profile page is displayed with all necessary elements such as name, email, and profile picture.
Step 5: Confirm that the navigation back to the home screen is straightforward by tapping the back button.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The profile page should be accessible via the menu, displaying all required elements clearly and allowing easy navigation back.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page data entry and validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the user can update their profile information with proper validation.
preconditions: User is logged in to the application
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Menu' icon located at the bottom of the screen.
Step 2: Scroll through the menu options until reaching the 'Profile' section.
Step 3: Tap on the 'Profile' option.
Step 4: Tap on the 'Edit Profile' button.
Step 5: Enter a new email address that is already in use and tap 'Save'.
Step 6: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the email is already taken.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: When attempting to save a duplicate email, an error message should be clearly displayed informing the user of the issue.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page performance and responsiveness
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the profile page loads quickly and responds to user interactions smoothly.
preconditions: User is logged in to the application
test_steps: Step 1: Tap on the 'Menu' icon located at the bottom of the screen.
Step 2: Scroll through the menu options until reaching the 'Profile' section.
Step 3: Tap on the 'Profile' option.
Step 4: Wait for the profile page to fully load and observe any delays or lag.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create a new user profile
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a new user profile can be created successfully.
preconditions: User is not logged in
test_steps: 1. Send POST request to /api/users with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include request body: {"username": "newuser", "email": "newuser@example.com", "password": "securepassword123"}
3. Verify HTTP 201 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains created user with auto-generated ID
5. Verify response body includes 'createdAt' timestamp
6. Verify user appears in GET /api/users response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User profile created successfully with auto-generated ID and timestamp.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Update user profile details
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user's profile details can be updated successfully.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send PUT request to /api/users/<user_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include request body: {"email": "updatedemail@example.com", "password": "newsecurepassword456"}
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains updated user details
5. Verify 'updatedAt' timestamp in response body is updated
6. Verify updated email and password appear in GET /api/users/<user_id> response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User profile details updated successfully with new email and password.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Delete user profile
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user's profile can be deleted successfully.
preconditions: User is logged in with valid JWT token
test_steps: 1. Send DELETE request to /api/users/<user_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 204 status code returned
3. Verify user no longer appears in GET /api/users response
4. Attempt to retrieve deleted user profile via GET /api/users/<user_id>
5. Verify HTTP 404 status code is returned
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User profile deleted successfully and cannot be retrieved.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create a new user profile with valid data

No existing user profiles with the same email

5. Click on the 'Submit' button
6. Confirm that a success message is displayed indicating profile creation

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during profile update
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from network interruptions while updating user profile information.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Profile page is open
test_steps: 1. Start updating the user's email address on the profile page
2. Enter a new email address and click 'Save'
3. Introduce a network interruption (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi)
4. Wait for the app to attempt saving the changes but fail due to the interruption
5. Re-establish the network connection
6. Verify that the app automatically retries the profile update and successfully saves the new email address
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should retry the profile update upon re-establishing network connection and save the new email address successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after profile update
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app can recover from crashes while updating user profile information.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Profile page is open
test_steps: 1. Start updating the user's phone number on the profile page
2. Enter a new phone number and click 'Save'
3. Introduce an app crash (e.g., force close the app)
4. Reopen the app and navigate to the profile page
5. Verify that the app loads the updated phone number from local storage
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should load the updated phone number from local storage upon reopening and not require re-entering the information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session timeout handling during profile view
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the app handles session timeouts gracefully while viewing user profile information.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Profile page is open
test_steps: 1. Start viewing the user's profile on the profile page
2. Wait for the session to timeout (e.g., 30 minutes of inactivity)
3. Attempt to interact with any element on the profile page
4. Verify that the app redirects the user to the login screen
5. Log back in and verify that the profile information is still accessible
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should redirect the user to the login screen upon session timeout and allow them to log back in without losing access to their profile information.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Load Test for Profile Page
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of loading the profile page under high user load.
preconditions: Application is running
User accounts are created
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to the application using a valid user account.
Step 2: Navigate to the Profile page.
Step 3: Simulate multiple users logging in and navigating to the Profile page simultaneously.
Step 4: Monitor the system's response time for each user accessing the Profile page.
Step 5: Record the number of successful logins and any errors encountered.
Step 6: Verify that the average response time does not exceed 2 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Average response time for accessing the Profile page should be less than or equal to 2 seconds with no more than 5% errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Concurrent Data Entry on Profile Page

User accounts are created

Step 2: Navigate to the Profile page.
Step 3: Simulate multiple users entering data (e.g., updating profile information) simultaneously.
Step 4: Monitor the system's response time for each user entering data.
Step 5: Record the number of successful data entries and any errors encountered.
Step 6: Verify that the average response time does not exceed 3 seconds.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Average response time for entering data on the Profile page should be less than or equal to 3 seconds with no more than 2% errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Stress Test for Profile Page
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the system's ability to handle a large number of users accessing and modifying the profile page simultaneously.
preconditions: Application is running
User accounts are created
test_steps: Step 1: Log in to the application using a valid user account.
Step 2: Navigate to the Profile page.
Step 3: Simulate a large number of users (e.g., 500) accessing and modifying the profile page simultaneously.
Step 4: Monitor the system's response time, CPU usage, memory usage, and error rates.
Step 5: Record the number of successful operations and any errors encountered.
Step 6: Verify that the average response time does not exceed 5 seconds, CPU usage is below 80%, memory usage is below 90%, and no more than 1% errors.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle up to 500 users accessing and modifying the Profile page simultaneously with an average response time of less than or equal to 5 seconds, CPU usage below 80%, memory usage below 90%, and no more than 1% errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all mandatory fields are validated correctly when creating a new profile.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Profile' page.
2. Click on the 'Edit Profile' button.
3. Attempt to save the profile without entering values in any mandatory fields (e.g., Name, Email).
4. Verify that an error message is displayed for each missing mandatory field indicating that it is required.
5. Enter valid values in all mandatory fields and attempt to save again.
6. Confirm that the profile is saved successfully without errors.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: All mandatory fields must display an error message when left empty and save operation should fail. Upon entering valid values, the profile should be saved successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of data type constraints in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the data types for specific fields are enforced correctly.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Profile' page.
2. Click on the 'Edit Profile' button.
3. Enter an invalid value in a field that expects a numeric type (e.g., Age) and attempt to save.
4. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the input must be a number.
5. Enter a valid numeric value in the same field and attempt to save again.
6. Confirm that the profile is saved successfully without errors.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Age' field must display an error message when non-numeric values are entered and save operation should fail. Upon entering a valid numeric value, the profile should be saved successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of foreign key relationships in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the foreign key relationships are enforced correctly when updating a profile.
preconditions: User is logged into the mobile application
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Profile' page.
2. Click on the 'Edit Profile' button.
3. Attempt to update the 'Country' field with an invalid value (e.g., a non-existent country code).
4. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the selected country does not exist.
5. Select a valid country from the dropdown and attempt to save again.
6. Confirm that the profile is saved successfully without errors.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The 'Country' field must display an error message when an invalid value is selected and save operation should fail. Upon selecting a valid country, the profile should be saved successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling during Profile Page Load

Profile data is available on the server

Step 3: Observe if the profile page loads successfully after the network is restored
Step 4: Verify that all user data (name, email, etc.) is displayed correctly upon reconnection
Step 5: Check if any error messages are shown during the interruption and resolution process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Profile page loads successfully after network restoration, displaying all user data without errors.

Profile data is available

Step 4: Observe if the profile page loads successfully without any errors
Step 5: Verify that all user data is intact and displayed correctly

Profile data is available

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page load time under high user concurrency
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the performance of the profile page when accessed by a large number of users simultaneously.
preconditions: Application is running in production environment
Database has sufficient data for testing
test_steps: Step 1: Start a load test using Apache JMeter with 50 concurrent users
Step 2: Monitor the application's response time and throughput during the test
Step 3: Ensure that all profile pages are loaded within 2 seconds for each user
Step 4: Verify that there are no errors or crashes in the application logs
Step 5: Scale up to 100 concurrent users and observe the performance impact
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Application should handle 50 concurrent users with a response time of less than 2 seconds per user, no errors or crashes in logs

Step 4: Verify that there are no errors or crashes in the application logs

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Profile page data retrieval under high load

phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user's profile details can be updated successfully.
preconditions: User is logged in
test_steps: 1. Send PUT request to /api/users/<user_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Include request body: {"email": "updatedemail@example.com", "password": "newsecurepassword456"}
3. Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains updated user details
5. Verify response body includes 'updatedAt' timestamp
6. Verify updated email and password appear in GET /api/users/<user_id> response
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User profile details are updated successfully with new email and password.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Delete user profile
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a user's profile can be deleted successfully.
preconditions: User is logged in
test_steps: 1. Send DELETE request to /api/users/<user_id> with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
2. Verify HTTP 204 status code returned
3. Verify user no longer appears in GET /api/users response
4. Attempt to retrieve deleted user profile via GET /api/users/<user_id>
5. Verify HTTP 404 status code is returned
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User profile is deleted successfully and cannot be retrieved.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of mandatory fields in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that all mandatory fields are validated correctly when updating a user profile.
preconditions: User is logged in
Profile page is accessible
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page and click on 'Edit' button.
2. Clear the value of the 'First Name' field.
3. Click on 'Save' button without entering any values.
4. Verify that an error message is displayed for the 'First Name' field indicating it is required.
5. Enter a valid first name and click on 'Save' button again.
6. Verify that the profile is updated successfully with the new first name.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for missing mandatory field, and successful update of profile with valid data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of email format in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the email field accepts only valid email addresses.
preconditions: User is logged in
Profile page is accessible
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page and click on 'Edit' button.
2. Enter an invalid email address (e.g., 'john@example') in the 'Email' field.
3. Click on 'Save' button.
4. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the email format is incorrect.
5. Enter a valid email address (e.g., 'john@example.com') and click on 'Save' button again.
6. Verify that the profile is updated successfully with the new valid email.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for invalid email format, and successful update of profile with valid email.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Validation of phone number length in Profile page
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure that the phone number field accepts only valid phone numbers with a specific length.
preconditions: User is logged in
Profile page is accessible
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the Profile page and click on 'Edit' button.
2. Enter an invalid phone number (e.g., '123456789') with less than 10 digits in the 'Phone Number' field.
3. Click on 'Save' button.
4. Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the phone number must be 10 digits long.
5. Enter a valid phone number (e.g., '1234567890') with exactly 10 digits and click on 'Save' button again.
6. Verify that the profile is updated successfully with the new valid phone number.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error message displayed for invalid phone number length, and successful update of profile with valid phone number.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network interruption handling during profile update
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify the application handles network interruptions gracefully when updating user profile information.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Device has internet connection
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile app and navigate to the Profile page.
2. Enter new email address 'newemail@example.com' in the Email field.
3. Tap on the Update button.
4. Simulate a network interruption by turning off the device's internet connection.
5. Observe if the application shows an error message indicating network issues and prevents further actions.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app displays a network error message and does not allow updating profile until internet is restored.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App crash recovery after profile update
phase2_test_cases.description: Ensure the application recovers gracefully from a crash during the profile update process.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Device has internet connection
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile app and navigate to the Profile page.
2. Enter new phone number '1234567890' in the Phone Number field.
3. Tap on the Update button.
4. Simulate an application crash by force stopping the app from the device's task manager.
5. Reopen the app and verify if the profile update is still in progress or if it has been reverted.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should recover from the crash and display a message indicating that the profile update was not completed. The user should be able to resume the update process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session timeout handling during profile view
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the application handles session timeouts gracefully when viewing user profile information.
preconditions: User is logged in with a valid session
Device has internet connection
test_steps: 1. Open the mobile app and navigate to the Profile page.
2. Wait for the session timeout period (e.g., 30 minutes) to expire.
3. Observe if the application automatically logs out the user and redirects them to the login screen.
4. Enter valid credentials and log in again.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should automatically log out the user after the session timeout period and redirect them to the login screen. The user should be able to log in successfully with their valid credentials.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create Account - High Load Test
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under high load when creating multiple accounts simultaneously.
preconditions: System is up and running
No existing users with usernames 'user1' to 'user1000'
Database has sufficient capacity
test_steps: Step 1: Start a load test using Apache JMeter or similar tool
Step 2: Configure the test plan to send concurrent requests for creating accounts
Step 3: Set up a loop controller to create 1000 accounts sequentially with usernames 'user1' to 'user1000'
Step 4: Monitor system metrics such as CPU, memory usage, and response times during the test
Step 5: Verify that all 1000 accounts are created successfully without errors
Step 6: Check database for the presence of all 1000 new users
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle 1000 concurrent account creations within 5 minutes with an average response time of less than 2 seconds per request.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create Account - Stress Test
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance under extreme load when creating a large number of accounts.
preconditions: System is up and running
No existing users with usernames 'user1' to 'user5000'
Database has sufficient capacity
test_steps: Step 1: Start a stress test using Apache JMeter or similar tool
Step 2: Configure the test plan to send concurrent requests for creating accounts
Step 3: Set up a loop controller to create 5000 accounts sequentially with usernames 'user1' to 'user5000'
Step 4: Monitor system metrics such as CPU, memory usage, and response times during the test
Step 5: Verify that all 5000 accounts are created successfully without errors
Step 6: Check database for the presence of all 5000 new users
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle 5000 concurrent account creations within 10 minutes with an average response time of less than 3 seconds per request.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create Account - Ramp-Up Test
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate system performance as the number of concurrent account creations increases over time.
preconditions: System is up and running
No existing users with usernames 'user1' to 'user200'
Database has sufficient capacity
test_steps: Step 1: Start a ramp-up test using Apache JMeter or similar tool
Step 2: Configure the test plan to send increasing numbers of concurrent requests for creating accounts over time
Step 3: Set up a loop controller to create accounts with usernames 'user1' to 'user200'
Step 4: Monitor system metrics such as CPU, memory usage, and response times during the test
Step 5: Verify that all accounts are created successfully without errors
Step 6: Check database for the presence of all new users
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: System should handle a gradual increase in concurrent account creations from 1 to 200 users without significant degradation in performance.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for account creation with invalid email format
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects account creation when an invalid email format is provided.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter an invalid email format in the 'Email' field (e.g., 'user@domain')
Step 2: Click on the 'Submit' button
Step 3: Verify that a validation error message is displayed under the 'Email' field
Step 4: Confirm that the error message states 'Please enter a valid email address'
Step 5: Verify that the account creation process does not proceed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message 'Please enter a valid email address' and prevent the account creation process.

test_data.expected_error_message: Please enter a valid email address

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for account creation with existing email
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects account creation when an email address that already exists is provided.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter an email address that is already registered (e.g., 'existinguser@example.com')
Step 2: Click on the 'Submit' button
Step 3: Verify that a validation error message is displayed under the 'Email' field
Step 4: Confirm that the error message states 'This email address is already in use'
Step 5: Verify that the account creation process does not proceed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message 'This email address is already in use' and prevent the account creation process.

test_data.expected_error_message: This email address is already in use

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Test for account creation with weak password
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the system rejects account creation when a weak password is provided.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a weak password in the 'Password' field (e.g., '123456')
Step 2: Click on the 'Submit' button
Step 3: Verify that a validation error message is displayed under the 'Password' field
Step 4: Confirm that the error message states 'Password must be at least 8 characters long and include both letters and numbers'
Step 5: Verify that the account creation process does not proceed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The system should display an error message 'Password must be at least 8 characters long and include both letters and numbers' and prevent the account creation process.

test_data.expected_error_message: Password must be at least 8 characters long and include both letters and numbers

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User can create an account with valid email and password
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the usability of the account creation process with correct input data.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a valid email address in the 'Email' field (e.g., testuser@example.com)
Step 2: Enter a password of at least 8 characters in the 'Password' field (e.g., TestPass123)
Step 3: Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field
Step 4: Click on the 'Create Account' button
Step 5: Verify that a success message is displayed indicating account creation was successful
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A success message is displayed within 5 seconds, confirming the account was created successfully.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User receives error message for invalid email format
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the usability of the account creation process with incorrect input data.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter an invalid email address in the 'Email' field (e.g., testuserexample.com)
Step 2: Enter a password of at least 8 characters in the 'Password' field
Step 3: Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field
Step 4: Click on the 'Create Account' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the email format is invalid
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed within 5 seconds, indicating the email format is invalid.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: User receives error message for mismatched passwords
phase2_test_cases.description: Validates the usability of the account creation process with incorrect input data.
preconditions: Navigate to the 'Create Account' page
test_steps: Step 1: Enter a valid email address in the 'Email' field
Step 2: Enter a password of at least 8 characters in the 'Password' field
Step 3: Confirm the password by entering a different value in the 'Confirm Password' field (e.g., TestPass456)
Step 4: Click on the 'Create Account' button
Step 5: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating the passwords do not match
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed within 5 seconds, indicating the passwords do not match.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account on iOS device with iPhone 12 and iOS 15
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the account creation process works correctly on an iPhone 12 running iOS 15.
preconditions: iPhone 12
iOS 15 installed
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app store and search for 'RestaurantApp'.
Step 2: Tap on the 'Get' button to download and install the RestaurantApp.
Step 3: Launch the RestaurantApp and tap on the 'Create Account' button.
Step 4: Enter a valid email address in the 'Email' field.
Step 5: Enter a strong password in the 'Password' field.
Step 6: Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field.
Step 7: Tap on the 'Create Account' button.
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The account is created successfully with a valid email and password, and the user is redirected to the home screen.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account on web browser with Chrome on Windows 10
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the account creation process works correctly using the RestaurantApp website in Google Chrome on a Windows 10 machine.

Google Chrome installed
test_steps: Step 1: Open Google Chrome and navigate to 'https://www.restaurantapp.com'.
Step 2: Click on the 'Sign Up' button located in the top right corner of the page.
Step 3: Enter a valid email address in the 'Email' field.

phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The account is created successfully with a valid email and password, and the user is redirected to the home page.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with valid credentials
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that a new user can be created successfully using valid email and password.
preconditions: No existing account with the provided email
test_steps: 1. Send POST request to /api/users/register with header Content-Type: application/json
2. Include request body: {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "Password123!"}
3. Verify HTTP 201 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains user details including auto-generated ID and email
5. Verify response body includes "createdAt" timestamp
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: User account is created successfully with a valid email and password.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with existing email
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to create an account with an already registered email returns an error.
preconditions: An existing account with the provided email
test_steps: 1. Send POST request to /api/users/register with header Content-Type: application/json
2. Include request body: {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "Password123!"}
3. Verify HTTP 409 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains error message indicating email already exists
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error is returned when attempting to create an account with an existing email.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with invalid password
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that attempting to create an account with an invalid password returns an error.
preconditions: No existing account with the provided email
test_steps: 1. Send POST request to /api/users/register with header Content-Type: application/json
2. Include request body: {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "weakpass"}
3. Verify HTTP 400 status code returned
4. Verify response body contains error message indicating password is too weak
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: Error is returned when attempting to create an account with a weak password.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with valid data
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that a new account can be created with all mandatory fields filled correctly.
preconditions: System is up and running
User has access to the registration page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Register' page
2. Enter valid email address in the 'Email' field
3. Enter a strong password in the 'Password' field
4. Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field
5. Click on the 'Create Account' button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: A new account is created successfully with the provided email and password, and a confirmation message is displayed.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with missing mandatory fields
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an error message is displayed when attempting to create an account without filling all mandatory fields.
preconditions: System is up and running
User has access to the registration page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Register' page
2. Leave the 'Email' field empty
3. Enter a strong password in the 'Password' field
4. Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field
5. Click on the 'Create Account' button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the email field cannot be empty.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Create account with invalid password format
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an error message is displayed when attempting to create an account with a password that does not meet the required criteria.
preconditions: System is up and running
User has access to the registration page
test_steps: 1. Navigate to the 'Register' page
2. Enter an email address in the 'Email' field
3. Enter a weak password (e.g., 'password') in the 'Password' field
4. Confirm the password by entering it again in the 'Confirm Password' field
5. Click on the 'Create Account' button
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the password does not meet the required criteria.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling During Account Creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the account creation process recovers gracefully after a network interruption.
preconditions: User is on a mobile device with internet access
App is open and ready to create an account
test_steps: Step 1: Start the account creation process by entering username 'john_doe' and password 'P@ssw0rd123'
Step 2: Enter email address 'john.doe@example.com'
Step 3: Tap on the 'Create Account' button
Step 4: Simulate a network interruption (e.g., turn off mobile data or airplane mode)
Step 5: Wait for the app to attempt to submit the account creation request and fail due to the network interruption
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display an error message indicating a network issue and allow the user to retry the account creation process.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App Crash Recovery During Account Creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the account creation process recovers gracefully after an app crash.
preconditions: User is on a mobile device with internet access
App is open and ready to create an account
test_steps: Step 1: Start the account creation process by entering username 'jane_doe' and password 'P@ssw0rd456'
Step 2: Enter email address 'jane.doe@example.com'
Step 3: Tap on the 'Create Account' button
Step 4: Simulate an app crash (e.g., force close the app)
Step 5: Reopen the app and attempt to complete the account creation process
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display a welcome screen and allow the user to continue with the account creation process where they left off.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data Persistence Across Crashes During Account Creation
phase2_test_cases.description: Verify that the account creation data persists across app crashes.
preconditions: User is on a mobile device with internet access
App is open and ready to create an account
test_steps: Step 1: Start the account creation process by entering username 'jimmy_doe' and password 'P@ssw0rd789'
Step 2: Enter email address 'jimmy.doe@example.com'
Step 3: Tap on the 'Create Account' button
Step 4: Simulate an app crash (e.g., force close the app)
Step 5: Reopen the app and verify that the account creation data (username, password, email) is still present in the form
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app should display the account creation form with the previously entered data still populated, allowing the user to complete the process without re-entering information.
